bet.name: bet
bet.function: Removes non-brain tissue from MRI images using a deformable surface model that iteratively fits to the brain boundary.
bet.modality: T1-weighted structural image (3D NIfTI). Can also process 4D fMRI with -F flag.
bet.keyParameters: -f (fractional intensity 0→1, default 0.5), -g (vertical gradient), -R (robust mode), -m (output binary mask)
bet.keyPoints: Default threshold works for most T1s. Use -R for noisy/difficult data. Lower -f (~0.3) for functional images.
bet.typicalUse: First step in structural or functional preprocessing to isolate brain tissue.

ngenerate.label: Do not generate the default output

radius.label: Radius of the brain centre in mm

threshold.label: Use thresholding to estimate the brain centre

mesh.label: Generate a mesh of the brain surface

exclusive.type: record(robust) | record(eye) | record(bias) | record(fov) | record(fmri) | record(betsurf) | record(betsurfT2)

fast.name: fast
fast.function: Segments brain images into gray matter, white matter, and CSF using a hidden Markov random field model with integrated bias field correction.
fast.keyParameters: -n (number of tissue classes, default 3), -t (image type: 1=T1, 2=T2, 3=PD), -B (output bias field), -o (output basename)
fast.keyPoints: Input must be brain-extracted. Outputs partial volume maps (*_pve_0/1/2) for each tissue class. Use -B to get estimated bias field.
fast.typicalUse: Tissue probability maps for normalization, VBM studies, or masking.

iterations.label: Number of iterations during bias-field removal

lowpass.label: bias field smoothing extent (FWHM) in mm

fhard.label: initial segmentation spatial smoothness (during bias field estimation)

segments.label: Outputs a separate binary segmentation file for each tissue type

fixed.label: number of main-loop iterations after bias-field removal

fslreorient2std.name: fslreorient2std
fslreorient2std.function: Reorients images to match standard (MNI) orientation using 90-degree rotations and flips only.
fslreorient2std.modality: 3D or 4D NIfTI volume in any orientation.
fslreorient2std.keyPoints: Only applies 90-degree rotations/flips (no interpolation). Does not register to standard space. Should be run as first step before any processing.

fslroi.name: fslroi
fslroi.function: Extracts a spatial or temporal sub-region from NIfTI images.
fslroi.keyPoints: Indices are 0-based. For temporal extraction only, use: fslroi input output tmin tsize. Useful for extracting reference volumes from 4D data.
fslroi.typicalUse: Cropping images spatially or selecting specific time points from 4D data.

t_size.label: Number of time points to extract

fslsplit.name: fslsplit
fslsplit.function: Splits a 4D time series into individual 3D volumes or splits along any spatial axis.
fslsplit.keyPoints: Default splits along time dimension. Output files are numbered sequentially (vol0000, vol0001, ...). Useful for per-volume quality control.
fslsplit.typicalUse: Processing individual volumes separately, quality control inspection.

dimension.label: Dimension to split along (t=time, x, y, z)

fslmerge.name: fslmerge
fslmerge.function: Concatenates multiple 3D volumes into a 4D time series or merges along any spatial axis.
fslmerge.keyPoints: Use -t for temporal concatenation (most common). -a auto-detects axis. Input images must have matching spatial dimensions when merging in time.
fslmerge.typicalUse: Combining processed volumes back into 4D, concatenating runs.

dimension.label: Dimension to merge along (t=time, x, y, z, a=auto)

tr.label: TR in seconds (when merging in time)

fslstats.name: fslstats
fslstats.function: Computes various summary statistics from image data, optionally within a mask region.
fslstats.keyParameters: -k (mask image), -m (mean), -s (standard deviation), -r (min max), -V (volume in voxels and mm3), -p (nth percentile)
fslstats.keyPoints: Apply -k mask before other options. Use -t for per-volume stats on 4D data. Outputs to stdout for easy scripting.
fslstats.typicalUse: Extracting summary statistics from ROIs or whole-brain.

split_4d.label: Generate separate statistics for each 3D volume

index_mask.label: Generate separate statistics for each integer label

robust_range.label: Output robust min and max (2nd and 98th percentiles)

std_nonzero.label: Output standard deviation of non-zero voxels

histogram.label: Output histogram with specified number of bins

abs_percentile.label: Output nth percentile of absolute values

cog_voxels.label: Output center of gravity in voxel coordinates

cog_mm.label: Output center of gravity in mm coordinates

fslmeants.name: fslmeants
fslmeants.function: Extracts the mean time series from a 4D dataset within a mask or at specified coordinates.
fslmeants.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI time series plus ROI mask or coordinates.
fslmeants.keyParameters: -i (input 4D), -o (output text file), -m (mask image), -c (x y z coordinates), --eig (output eigenvariates)
fslmeants.keyPoints: Outputs one value per timepoint. Use -m for mask-based extraction, -c for single-voxel. --eig outputs eigenvariate (first principal component) instead of mean.
fslmeants.typicalUse: ROI time series extraction for seed-based connectivity analysis.

label.label: Label image for extracting timeseries per label

coord.label: Voxel coordinates (x y z) for single voxel timeseries

usemm.label: Use mm coordinates instead of voxel coordinates

showall.label: Show all voxel time series instead of mean

transpose.label: Output in transposed format (time x voxels)

nobin.label: Do not binarize mask for eigenvariate calculation

cluster.name: cluster
cluster.function: Identifies contiguous clusters of suprathreshold voxels in statistical images and reports their properties.
cluster.keyParameters: -i (input stat image), -t (z threshold), -p (p threshold), --oindex (cluster index output), --olmax (local maxima output), -c (cope image for effect sizes)
cluster.keyPoints: Reports cluster size, peak coordinates, and p-values. Use with -c to get mean COPE within clusters. GRF-based p-values require smoothness estimates.
cluster.typicalUse: Cluster-based thresholding and extracting peak coordinates from statistical maps.

cope.label: COPE image for effect size reporting

peakdist.label: Minimum distance between peaks in mm

fractional.label: Interpret threshold as fraction of robust range

mm.label: Use mm coordinates instead of voxel

num_maxima.label: Number of local maxima to report

stdvol.label: Standard space volume for coordinate transformation

mcflirt.name: mcflirt
mcflirt.function: Intra-modal motion correction for fMRI time series using rigid-body (6-DOF) transformations optimized for fMRI data.
mcflirt.keyParameters: -refvol (reference volume index), -cost (cost function), -plots (output motion parameter plots), -mats (save transformation matrices)
mcflirt.keyPoints: Default reference is middle volume. Use -plots for motion parameter files (6 columns: 3 rotations + 3 translations). Motion params useful as nuisance regressors.
mcflirt.typicalUse: Correcting head motion in functional data; motion parameters used as nuisance regressors.

ref_vol.label: Reference volume number (default is middle volume)

ref_file.label: External reference image for motion correction

stats.label: Produce variance and std dev images

smooth.label: Smoothing for cost function (FWHM in mm)

slicetimer.name: slicetimer
slicetimer.function: Corrects for differences in slice acquisition times within each volume using sinc interpolation.
slicetimer.keyParameters: -r (TR in seconds), --odd (interleaved odd slices first), --down (reverse slice order), --tcustom (custom timing file)
slicetimer.keyPoints: Must match actual acquisition order. Important for event-related designs with short TRs. Less critical for long TRs or block designs.
slicetimer.typicalUse: Temporal alignment of slices acquired at different times within each TR.

global_shift.label: Global shift as fraction of TR (0-1, default 0.5)

susan.name: susan
susan.function: Edge-preserving noise reduction using nonlinear filtering that smooths within tissue boundaries while preserving edges.
susan.keyPoints: Brightness threshold typically 0.75 * median intensity. Set dimensionality to 3 for 3D volumes. Better edge preservation than Gaussian smoothing.
susan.typicalUse: Noise reduction while preserving structural boundaries in functional or structural data.

brightness_threshold.label: Brightness threshold (should be > noise and < edge contrast)

use_median.label: Use median (1) or mean (0) for brightness calculation (default 1)

n_usans.label: Number of USAN areas to use (0, 1, or 2)

flirt.name: flirt
flirt.function: Linear (affine) registration between images using optimized cost functions with 6, 7, 9, or 12 degrees of freedom.
flirt.modality: Any 3D NIfTI volume pair (structural, functional, or standard template).
flirt.keyPoints: Use 6-DOF for within-subject rigid-body, 12-DOF for cross-subject affine. Cost function matters: corratio for intra-modal, mutualinfo for inter-modal.

apply_isoxfm.label: Apply transformation and resample to isotropic voxels

search_cost.label: Search cost function (if different from cost)

applywarp.name: applywarp
applywarp.function: Applies linear and/or non-linear warp fields to transform images between coordinate spaces.
applywarp.modality: 3D or 4D NIfTI volume plus warp field and/or affine matrix.
applywarp.keyPoints: Can chain affine + nonlinear transforms in one step. Use --interp=nn for label images, --interp=spline for continuous images. Reference defines output grid.
applywarp.typicalUse: Applying normalization warps to functional data or atlas labels.

relwarp.label: Treat warp as relative (x' = x + w(x))

invwarp.name: invwarp
invwarp.function: Computes the inverse of a non-linear warp field for reverse transformations.
invwarp.modality: Non-linear warp field (4D NIfTI from FNIRT --cout output).
invwarp.keyParameters: -w (input warp), -o (output inverse warp), -r (reference image for output space)
invwarp.keyPoints: Needed to map atlas/standard-space ROIs back to native space. Reference should be the image that was originally warped.
invwarp.typicalUse: Creating inverse transformations for atlas-to-native space mapping.

convertwarp.name: convertwarp
convertwarp.function: Combines multiple warp fields and affine matrices into a single composite warp for efficient one-step transformation.
convertwarp.modality: Warp fields and/or affine matrices from FLIRT/FNIRT.
convertwarp.keyPoints: Applying one combined warp is faster and has less interpolation error than chaining multiple transformations. Transform order: premat > warp1 > midmat > warp2 > postmat.
convertwarp.typicalUse: Concatenating multiple transformations (e.g., func > struct > standard) efficiently.

fslmaths.name: fslmaths
fslmaths.function: Performs a wide range of voxelwise mathematical operations on NIfTI images including arithmetic, filtering, thresholding, and morphological operations.
fslmaths.keyPoints: Swiss army knife of neuroimaging. Operations are applied left to right. Use -odt to control output data type. -bptf values are in volumes not seconds.
fslmaths.typicalUse: Mathematical operations, masking, thresholding, temporal filtering.

thrp.label: Threshold below percentage of robust range

thrP.label: Threshold below percentage of non-zero voxels

uthrp.label: Upper threshold percentage of robust range

uthrP.label: Upper threshold percentage of non-zero voxels

fmeanu.label: Mean filter using non-zero neighbors only

bptf.label: Bandpass temporal filter (hp_sigma lp_sigma in volumes)

fnirt.name: fnirt
fnirt.function: Non-linear registration using B-spline deformations for precise anatomical alignment to a template.
fnirt.modality: T1-weighted 3D NIfTI volume plus reference template. Requires initial affine from FLIRT.
fnirt.keyPoints: Always run FLIRT first for initial alignment. Use --config=T1_2_MNI152_2mm for standard T1-to-MNI. Computationally intensive.
fnirt.typicalUse: High-accuracy normalization to MNI space for group analyses.

intin.label: Initial intensity mapping from previous FNIRT run

ssqlambda.label: Weight lambda by sum-of-squared differences (0 or 1)

fugue.name: fugue
fugue.fullName: FMRIB's Utility for Geometrically Unwarping EPIs (FUGUE)
fugue.function: Corrects geometric distortions in EPI images caused by magnetic field inhomogeneity using acquired fieldmap data.
fugue.modality: 3D/4D EPI NIfTI plus preprocessed fieldmap (in rad/s).
fugue.keyPoints: Requires preprocessed fieldmap (e.g., from fsl_prepare_fieldmap). Dwell time and unwarp direction must match acquisition parameters.
fugue.typicalUse: Distortion correction when fieldmap data is available.

phasemap.label: Unwrapped phase image (or two-echo phase difference)

asym.label: Asymmetry time (echo time difference) in ms

noextend.label: Do not extend shifted voxels outside FOV

topup.name: topup
topup.fullName: Tool for Estimating and Correcting Susceptibility-Induced Distortions (TOPUP)
topup.function: Estimates and corrects susceptibility-induced distortions using pairs of images with reversed phase-encode directions.
topup.modality: 4D NIfTI with concatenated blip-up/blip-down b=0 images, plus acquisition parameters file.
topup.keyPoints: Requires reversed phase-encode image pair. Default config b02b0.cnf works well for most data. Outputs warp fields reusable by applytopup.
topup.typicalUse: Distortion correction using blip-up/blip-down acquisitions for fMRI or DWI.

input.label: Input 4D image with reversed phase-encode pairs

encoding_file.label: Acquisition parameters file (phase encode direction and readout time)

film_gls.name: film_gls
film_gls.function: Fits general linear model to fMRI time series with prewhitening using autocorrelation correction.
film_gls.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI time series plus design matrix and contrast files.
film_gls.keyPoints: Core statistical engine of FEAT. Design matrix must be pre-generated (e.g., via Feat_model). When --con is provided, directly computes COPEs, VARCOPEs, T-statistics, and Z-statistics alongside parameter estimates (pe) and residuals.
film_gls.typicalUse: First-level statistical analysis within FEAT or standalone.

feat.name: feat
feat.function: Complete fMRI analysis pipeline combining preprocessing (motion correction, spatial smoothing, temporal filtering), first-level statistical modeling (GLM with autocorrelation correction), and higher-level group analysis.
feat.modality: FEAT design file (.fsf) encoding all analysis parameters including input data paths, preprocessing options, design matrix, and contrasts.
feat.keyParameters: design_file (.fsf file with all configuration), input_data (4D BOLD data referenced by the .fsf)
feat.keyPoints: Pipeline controlled by the .fsf file. input_data must be provided separately for containerized execution (paths are rewritten at runtime). Internally runs BET, MCFLIRT, spatial smoothing, film_gls (with --con for contrast computation), registration, and optionally higher-level analysis. Outputs a .feat directory.
feat.typicalUse: Complete first-level or higher-level fMRI analysis when all parameters are pre-configured in an FSF file.

design_file.label: FEAT design file (.fsf) containing all analysis parameters

input_data.label: 4D BOLD input data referenced by the design file

flameo.name: flameo
flameo.fullName: FMRIB's Local Analysis of Mixed Effects (FLAME)
flameo.function: Group-level mixed-effects analysis accounting for both within-subject and between-subject variance using MCMC-based Bayesian estimation.
flameo.modality: 4D NIfTI of stacked subject-level COPEs, VARCOPEs, plus group design matrix and contrast files.
flameo.keyPoints: FLAME1 is recommended (good accuracy with reasonable speed). OLS is fast but ignores within-subject variance. FLAME1+2 is most accurate but slowest.
flameo.typicalUse: Second-level group analyses with proper random effects.

run_mode.label: Inference mode (fe=fixed effects, ols=OLS, flame1=FLAME stage 1, flame12=FLAME stages 1+2)

randomise.name: randomise
randomise.function: Non-parametric permutation testing for statistical inference with multiple correction methods including TFCE.
randomise.modality: 4D NIfTI of stacked subject images plus design matrix and contrast files.
randomise.keyPoints: Use -T for TFCE (recommended). 5000+ permutations for publication. Computationally intensive but provides strong family-wise error control.
randomise.typicalUse: Group-level inference with family-wise error correction (VBM, TBSS, etc.).

p_vec_n_dist_files.label: Output permutation vector and null distribution

show_total_perms.label: Print total number of permutations and exit

melodic.name: melodic
melodic.fullName: Multivariate Exploratory Linear Optimized Decomposition into Independent Components (MELODIC)
melodic.function: Multi-step ICA pipeline that decomposes fMRI data into spatially independent components. Internally chains brain masking, variance normalization, and probabilistic ICA decomposition.
melodic.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI time series (single-subject or concatenated multi-subject).
melodic.keyPoints: Auto-dimensionality estimation by default (Laplace approximation). Can be run single-subject or group. Components classified as signal vs. noise manually or via FIX.
melodic.typicalUse: Data exploration, artifact identification, resting-state network analysis.

input_files.label: Input file(s) - single 4D file or list of files

dim.label: Number of dimensions to reduce to (default automatic)

approach.label: ICA approach (2D: defl/symm, 3D: tica, group: concat)

pbsc.label: Switch off percent BOLD signal change conversion

num_ICs.label: Number of ICs to extract (deflation approach)

ICs.label: IC components file for mixture modelling

out_stats.label: Output thresholded maps and probability maps

report_maps.label: Control string for spatial map images

log_power.label: Calculate log of power for frequency spectrum

dual_regression.name: dual_regression
dual_regression.function: Multi-step pipeline that projects group-level ICA spatial maps back to individual subjects. Internally chains spatial regression (stage 1), temporal regression (stage 2), and optionally randomise permutation testing (stage 3).
dual_regression.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI time series for each subject plus group ICA spatial maps.
dual_regression.keyPoints: Two-stage regression: (1) spatial regression gives subject time courses, (2) temporal regression gives subject spatial maps. Can include randomise for group comparison.
dual_regression.typicalUse: Subject-level ICA-based resting-state network analysis and group comparisons.

input_files.label: List of input 4D files (one per subject)

run_first_all.name: run_first_all
run_first_all.fullName: FMRIB's Integrated Registration and Segmentation Tool (FIRST)
run_first_all.function: Multi-step pipeline for subcortical structure segmentation. Internally chains registration, boundary correction, and shape-model segmentation for 15 subcortical structures.
run_first_all.modality: T1-weighted 3D NIfTI volume (does not need to be brain-extracted).
run_first_all.keyParameters: -i (input image), -o (output basename), -b (run BET first), -s (comma-separated structures list)
run_first_all.keyPoints: Models 15 subcortical structures. Outputs meshes (.vtk) and volumetric labels. Can run on selected structures only with -s flag.
run_first_all.typicalUse: Volumetric analysis of subcortical structures (hippocampus, amygdala, caudate, etc.).

structures.label: Run only on specified structures (comma-separated list)

affine.label: Use this affine matrix for registration

sienax.name: sienax
sienax.function: Multi-step pipeline for cross-sectional brain volume estimation. Internally chains BET brain extraction, atlas registration, FAST tissue segmentation, and head-size-normalized volume computation.
sienax.keyPoints: Single timepoint analysis. Normalizes volumes by head size for cross-subject comparisons. Reports total brain, GM, and WM volumes.
sienax.typicalUse: Single timepoint normalized brain volume measures.

regional.label: Estimate regional volumes (peripheral GM, ventricular CSF)

lesion_mask.label: Lesion mask to correct mislabeled GM voxels

top_threshold.label: Ignore from this height (mm) upwards in MNI space

bottom_threshold.label: Ignore from this height (mm) downwards in MNI space

siena.name: siena
siena.fullName: Structural Image Evaluation using Normalisation of Atrophy (SIENA)
siena.function: Multi-step pipeline that estimates percentage brain volume change between two timepoints. Internally chains BET brain extraction, registration, FAST segmentation, and edge-point displacement analysis.
siena.modality: Two T1-weighted 3D NIfTI volumes from different timepoints.
siena.keyPoints: Requires two scans of same subject at different timepoints. Reports percentage brain volume change (PBVC). Accurate to ~0.2% volume change.
siena.typicalUse: Measuring brain volume change over time (e.g., atrophy in neurodegeneration).

std_masking.label: Apply standard-space masking for difficult cases

top_threshold.label: Ignore from this height (mm) upwards in MNI space

bottom_threshold.label: Ignore from this height (mm) downwards in MNI space

fsl_anat.name: fsl_anat
fsl_anat.function: Comprehensive automated pipeline for structural T1 processing including reorientation, cropping, bias correction, registration, segmentation, and subcortical segmentation.
fsl_anat.keyPoints: Runs BET, FAST, FLIRT, FNIRT, and FIRST in sequence. Creates output directory with all intermediate files. Good for standardized structural processing.
fsl_anat.typicalUse: Full structural preprocessing from T1 image.

nosearch.label: Do not search for existing .anat directory

probtrackx2.name: probtrackx2
probtrackx2.function: Probabilistic tractography using fiber orientation distributions from bedpostx to trace white matter pathways.
probtrackx2.modality: BEDPOSTX output directory plus seed mask (3D NIfTI).
probtrackx2.keyParameters: -x (seed mask), -s (bedpostx merged samples), --dir (output directory), -l (loop check), --waypoints (waypoint masks), --avoid (exclusion mask)
probtrackx2.keyPoints: Requires bedpostx output. Use --omatrix1 for seed-to-voxel connectivity, --omatrix2 for NxN connectivity. Waypoints constrain tractography to specific paths.
probtrackx2.typicalUse: White matter connectivity analysis, tract-based statistics.

samples_dir.label: bedpostX output directory containing merged samples

seed.label: Seed volume or text file of coordinates

onewaycondition.label: Apply waypoint conditions to each direction separately

waypoints.label: Waypoint mask (paths must pass through ALL)

pd.label: Output path distribution for each target

network.label: Network mode (seed file is list of seeds)

3dSkullStrip.name: 3dSkullStrip
3dSkullStrip.function: Removes non-brain tissue using a modified spherical surface expansion algorithm adapted from BET.
3dSkullStrip.keyPoints: Often more aggressive than BET. Use -push_to_edge if too much brain is removed. Works on T1 or T2 images.
3dSkullStrip.typicalUse: Brain extraction for structural or functional images in AFNI pipelines.

mask_vol.label: Output mask volume instead of skull-stripped volume

fill_hole.label: Fill holes up to R pixels

3dvolreg.name: 3dvolreg
3dvolreg.function: Rigid-body motion correction by registering all volumes in a 4D dataset to a base volume.
3dvolreg.keyParameters: -base (reference volume index or dataset), -prefix (output), -1Dfile (motion parameters output), -maxdisp1D (max displacement output)
3dvolreg.keyPoints: Default base is volume 0; use median volume for better results. Motion parameters output as 6 columns.
3dvolreg.typicalUse: Motion correction for fMRI; outputs 6 motion parameters for nuisance regression.

dfile.label: Save 9-column motion parameters (roll, pitch, yaw, dS, dL, dP, rmsold, rmsnew)

clipit.label: Clip output values to input range (default)

maxdisp.label: Print max brain voxel displacement in mm

maxdisp1D.label: Write max displacement per sub-brick to file

zpad.label: Zero-pad by n voxels during rotations (default 4)

weight.label: Apply voxel weighting from specified brick

3dTshift.name: 3dTshift
3dTshift.function: Corrects for slice timing differences by shifting each voxel time series to a common temporal reference.
3dTshift.keyParameters: -prefix (output), -tpattern (slice timing pattern: alt+z, seq+z, etc.), -tzero (align to time zero), -TR (repetition time)
3dTshift.keyPoints: Auto-detects slice timing from header if available. Common patterns: alt+z (interleaved ascending), seq+z (sequential ascending).
3dTshift.typicalUse: Aligning all slices to the same temporal reference in fMRI data.

tzero.label: Align each slice to this time offset

slice.label: Align to temporal offset of this slice number

tpattern.label: Slice timing pattern (alt+z, alt-z, seq+z, seq-z, @filename)

rlt.label: Remove mean and linear trend from output

rlt_plus.label: Remove trend then restore only mean to output

no_detrend.label: Do not remove or restore linear trend

voxshift.label: Dataset with voxel-wise shift fractions per TR

verbose.label: Print lots of messages while program runs

3dDespike.name: 3dDespike
3dDespike.function: Removes transient signal spikes from fMRI time series using an L1-norm fitting approach.
3dDespike.keyPoints: Run early in preprocessing pipeline (before motion correction). -NEW algorithm recommended.
3dDespike.typicalUse: Artifact removal before other preprocessing steps.

ignore.label: Ignore the first I points in the time series

nomask.label: Process all voxels instead of using auto-mask

NEW.label: Use faster fitting method for long series

3dBandpass.name: 3dBandpass
3dBandpass.function: Applies temporal bandpass filtering to fMRI time series with optional simultaneous nuisance regression.
3dBandpass.keyPoints: Typical resting-state range: 0.01-0.1 Hz. Can simultaneously regress nuisance signals with -ort.

fbot.label: Lowest frequency in passband (Hz), can be 0 for lowpass

despike.label: Remove spikes from time series before processing

nodetrend.label: Skip quadratic detrending before FFT bandpassing

ort.label: Orthogonalize input to columns in 1D file

dsort.label: Orthogonalize each voxel to matching voxel in dataset

dt.label: Set time step in seconds (default from header)

blur.label: Apply spatial filtering with specified FWHM (mm)

localPV.label: Replace vectors with local principal vector (radius in mm)

norm.label: Normalize output time series to L2 norm = 1

3dBlurToFWHM.name: 3dBlurToFWHM
3dBlurToFWHM.fullName: AFNI 3D Adaptive Smoothing to Target FWHM
3dBlurToFWHM.function: Adaptively smooths data to achieve a target smoothness level, accounting for existing smoothness.
3dBlurToFWHM.keyPoints: Measures existing smoothness and adds only enough to reach target FWHM. Better than fixed-kernel smoothing.

FWHMxy.label: Target 2D (x,y)-plane FWHM in mm (no z-axis blurring)

mask.label: Mask dataset limiting blurring to masked voxels

automask.label: Generate automatic mask from input dataset

ACF.label: Use autocorrelation function method for smoothness estimation

detin.label: Detrend input dataset before and after blurring

3dmerge.name: 3dmerge
3dmerge.function: Combines spatial filtering and dataset merging operations, commonly used for Gaussian smoothing.
3dmerge.keyPoints: Simple Gaussian smoothing with -1blur_fwhm. -doall applies to all volumes in 4D.

thtoin.label: Copy threshold data over intensity data

clip.label: Clip intensities in range (-val,val) to zero

thresh.label: Use threshold data to censor intensities

clust_mean.label: Replace cluster voxels with average intensity

clust_max.label: Replace cluster voxels with maximum intensity

filter_mean.label: Set each voxel to average intensity within radius (mm)

erode.label: Set voxel to zero unless pv% of nearby voxels are nonzero

dilate.label: Restore voxels removed by erosion if neighbors exist

dindex.label: Use sub-brick j as data source

tindex.label: Use sub-brick k as threshold source

doall.label: Apply options to all sub-bricks uniformly

nozero.label: Do not write output if all zero

3dAllineate.name: 3dAllineate
3dAllineate.function: Linear (affine) registration with multiple cost functions and optimization methods.
3dAllineate.keyParameters: -source (moving image), -base (reference), -prefix (output), -cost (cost function: lpc, mi, nmi), -1Dmatrix_save (save transform)
3dAllineate.keyPoints: lpc cost recommended for EPI-to-T1 alignment. nmi for intra-modal.
3dAllineate.typicalUse: Affine alignment between modalities or to standard space.

twopass.label: Apply two-pass strategy to all sub-bricks

twobest.label: Number of best coarse candidates for fine pass (0-29, default 5)

twoblur.label: Blurring radius for coarse pass in mm (default 11)

fineblur.label: Blurring radius for fine pass in mm (default 0)

autoweight.label: Compute weight from automask plus blurring

master.label: Output uses grid of specified dataset

EPI.label: Treat source as EPI with constrained warping

cmass.label: Use center-of-mass for initial shift alignment

3dQwarp.name: 3dQwarp
3dQwarp.function: Non-linear registration using cubic polynomial basis functions for precise anatomical alignment.
3dQwarp.modality: T1-weighted 3D NIfTI/AFNI volumes (source and base, both skull-stripped).
3dQwarp.keyPoints: Both images should be skull-stripped. Use -blur 0 3 for typical T1 registration. Usually preceded by 3dAllineate.

blur.label: Gaussian smoothing in voxels FWHM (one or two values)

pblur.label: Progressive blurring scaled with patch size

nowarp.label: Do not save the warp transformation

nodset.label: Do not save warped source dataset

3dUnifize.name: 3dUnifize
3dUnifize.function: Corrects intensity inhomogeneity (bias field) to produce uniform white matter intensity.
3dUnifize.keyPoints: Fast bias correction alternative to N4. Works well for T1 images by default. Use -T2 flag for T2-weighted images.
3dUnifize.typicalUse: Bias correction before segmentation or registration.

EPI.label: Process T2/T2* weighted EPI time series

GM.label: Scale to unifize gray matter intensities

Urad.label: Radius in voxels for processing ball (default 18.3)

nosquash.label: Disable reduction of large intensity values

ssave.label: Save white matter scale factors to dataset

3dAutomask.name: 3dAutomask
3dAutomask.function: Creates a brain mask automatically from EPI data by finding connected high-intensity voxels.
3dAutomask.keyParameters: -prefix (output mask), -dilate (number of dilation steps), -erode (number of erosion steps), -clfrac (clip fraction)
3dAutomask.keyPoints: Works on EPI data directly (no structural needed). Lower -clfrac includes more voxels.
3dAutomask.typicalUse: Generating functional brain masks from EPI data.

nograd.label: Use fixed clip level instead of gradual method

peels.label: Erode then dilate mask N times to remove thin protrusions (default 1)

dilate.label: Dilate the mask outwards N times

erode.label: Erode the mask inwards N times

NN2.label: Use face and edge neighbors (18 neighbors, default)

NN3.label: Use face, edge, and corner neighbors (26 neighbors)

nbhrs.label: Number of neighbors needed to not erode (6-26, default 17)

eclip.label: Remove exterior voxels below the clip threshold

SI.label: Zero out voxels more than N mm inferior to superior voxel

apply_prefix.label: Apply mask to input and save masked dataset

depth.label: Produce dataset showing peel operations to reach each voxel

3dTcat.name: 3dTcat
3dTcat.function: Concatenates datasets along the time dimension or selects specific sub-bricks from 4D data.
3dTcat.keyPoints: Sub-brick selectors allow flexible volume selection: [0..5] for first 6, [0..$-3] to skip last 3.
3dTcat.typicalUse: Combining runs, removing initial steady-state volumes.

glueto.label: Append bricks to the end of this dataset

rlt.label: Remove linear trends in each voxel time series

rlt_plus.label: Remove trends while restoring individual dataset means

rlt_plusplus.label: Remove trends while restoring overall mean

relabel.label: Replace sub-brick labels with input dataset name

tr.label: Repetition time in seconds for output dataset

@auto_tlrc.name: @auto_tlrc
@auto_tlrc.function: Automated Talairach transformation using affine registration to a template atlas.
@auto_tlrc.keyPoints: Legacy tool for Talairach normalization. For modern analyses, prefer @SSwarper or 3dQwarp.

base.label: Reference template in standard space (e.g., TT_N27+tlrc)

no_ss.label: Do not strip skull of input dataset

warp_orig_vol.label: Preserve skull in output by warping original volume

dxyz.label: Cubic voxel size in mm (default matches template)

pad_base.label: Padding in mm to prevent cropping (default 15)

init_xform.label: Apply preliminary affine transform before registration

@SSwarper.name: @SSwarper
@SSwarper.fullName: AFNI Skull Strip and Nonlinear Warp (@SSwarper)
@SSwarper.function: Combined skull stripping and non-linear warping to template in a single optimized pipeline.
@SSwarper.modality: T1-weighted 3D NIfTI volume plus reference template.
@SSwarper.keyPoints: Preferred over separate skull-strip + registration. Output compatible with afni_proc.py.
@SSwarper.typicalUse: Modern anatomical preprocessing for afni_proc.py pipelines.

base.label: Base template dataset with similar contrast

subid.label: Subject ID code for output datasets

skipwarp.label: Stop after skull-stripping, skip warping to template

ceil_off.label: Skip ceiling value capping at 98th percentile

cost_nl_init.label: Cost function for initial nonlinear alignment (default lpa)

cost_nl_final.label: Cost function for final nonlinear alignment (default pcl)

giant_move.label: Apply expanded parameter alignment for extreme angles

mask_ss.label: Provide mask instead of performing skull-stripping

align_epi_anat.name: align_epi_anat
align_epi_anat.function: Aligns EPI functional images to anatomical images with optional distortion correction using local Pearson correlation.
align_epi_anat.modality: EPI volume (3D NIfTI) plus T1-weighted anatomical.
align_epi_anat.keyParameters: -epi (EPI dataset), -anat (anatomical), -epi_base (EPI reference volume), -cost (cost function, default lpc)
align_epi_anat.keyPoints: lpc cost function designed for EPI-to-T1 alignment. Central tool in afni_proc.py.
align_epi_anat.typicalUse: Core EPI-to-structural alignment in functional preprocessing.

suffix.label: Suffix to append to output names (default _al)

big_move.label: Enable two-pass alignment for larger displacements

giant_move.label: Even larger movement - uses cmass, two passes, very large angles

rigid_body.label: Limit transformation to translation and rotation only

volreg.label: Perform volume registration on EPI (default on)

edge.label: Use edge-detection method instead of standard cost

3dDeconvolve.name: 3dDeconvolve
3dDeconvolve.function: Multiple linear regression analysis for fMRI with flexible hemodynamic response function models.
3dDeconvolve.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI/AFNI time series plus stimulus timing files.
3dDeconvolve.keyParameters: -input (4D data), -polort (polynomial detrending order), -num_stimts (number of regressors), -stim_times (timing files with HRF model), -gltsym (contrasts)
3dDeconvolve.keyPoints: Supports many HRF models (GAM, BLOCK, dmBLOCK, TENT, CSPLIN). Use -x1D_stop to generate design matrix only.
3dDeconvolve.typicalUse: First-level GLM analysis with flexible HRF models.

polort.label: Polynomial degree for baseline (default 1, 'A' for auto)

ortvec.label: Baseline vectors from file as nuisance regressors

local_times.label: Interpret stimulus times relative to run starts

global_times.label: Interpret stimulus times relative to first run

censor.label: Censor file for excluding time points

3dREMLfit.name: 3dREMLfit
3dREMLfit.function: GLM with ARMA(1,1) temporal autocorrelation correction using restricted maximum likelihood estimation.
3dREMLfit.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI/AFNI time series plus design matrix from 3dDeconvolve.
3dREMLfit.keyPoints: More accurate statistics than 3dDeconvolve OLS. Run after 3dDeconvolve for improved inference.
3dREMLfit.typicalUse: More accurate first-level statistics than 3dDeconvolve OLS.

STATmask.label: Separate mask for FDR curve computation

Rbuck.label: Output betas and statistics from REML

Rbeta.label: Save beta weights from REML estimation

Rvar.label: Save ARMA parameters and variance estimates

usetemp.label: Write intermediate data to disk (reduces RAM)

3dttest++.name: 3dttest++
3dttest++.function: Two-sample t-test with support for covariates, paired tests, and cluster-level inference.
3dttest++.keyPoints: Use -Clustsim for built-in cluster-level correction. -covariates allows continuous covariates.

labelA.label: Name for set A in output labels

labelB.label: Name for set B in output labels

unpooled.label: Compute variance separately for each set

BminusA.label: Reverse subtraction order to B - A

rankize.label: Convert data and covariates into ranks

zskip.label: Skip zero values in voxel analysis

exblur.label: Apply additional Gaussian smoothing (FWHM in mm)

ETAC.label: Multi-threshold clustering with equitable FPR control

singletonA.label: Test single value against group mean

3dANOVA.name: 3dANOVA
3dANOVA.function: Voxelwise fixed-effects one-way analysis of variance.
3dANOVA.modality: Multiple 3D NIfTI/AFNI volumes organized by factor level.
3dANOVA.keyParameters: -levels (number of levels), -dset (level dataset), -ftr (F-test output), -mean (level means output)
3dANOVA.keyPoints: Fixed-effects only. For random/mixed effects, use 3dMVM or 3dLME instead.

ftr.label: F-statistic for treatment effect output prefix

mean.label: Estimate of factor level mean (level prefix pairs)

diff.label: Difference between factor levels (level1 level2 prefix)

3dANOVA2.name: 3dANOVA2
3dANOVA2.function: Voxelwise fixed-effects two-way analysis of variance with main effects and interaction.
3dANOVA2.modality: Multiple 3D NIfTI/AFNI volumes organized by two factors.
3dANOVA2.keyPoints: Type determines fixed/random effects per factor. Types 1-3 for within-subject designs.

type.label: ANOVA model type (1=random A, 2=random B, 3=both fixed)

alevels.label: Number of levels for factor A

blevels.label: Number of levels for factor B

amean.label: Mean for level of factor A (level prefix)

bmean.label: Mean for level of factor B (level prefix)

adiff.label: Difference between levels of A (level1 level2 prefix)

bdiff.label: Difference between levels of B (level1 level2 prefix)

3dANOVA3.name: 3dANOVA3
3dANOVA3.function: Voxelwise fixed-effects three-way analysis of variance.
3dANOVA3.modality: Multiple 3D NIfTI/AFNI volumes organized by three factors.
3dANOVA3.keyPoints: Extension of 3dANOVA2 to three factors. Consider 3dMVM for more flexible modeling.

type.label: ANOVA model type (1-5 for different random/fixed combinations)

alevels.label: Number of levels for factor A

blevels.label: Number of levels for factor B

clevels.label: Number of levels for factor C

fab.label: F-statistic for A x B interaction

fac.label: F-statistic for A x C interaction

fbc.label: F-statistic for B x C interaction

fabc.label: F-statistic for A x B x C interaction

amean.label: Mean for level of factor A

bmean.label: Mean for level of factor B

cmean.label: Mean for level of factor C

3dClustSim.name: 3dClustSim
3dClustSim.function: Simulates null distribution of cluster sizes for determining cluster-extent thresholds that control family-wise error rate.
3dClustSim.modality: Brain mask (3D NIfTI/AFNI) plus smoothness estimates from 3dFWHMx.
3dClustSim.keyParameters: -mask (brain mask), -acf (ACF parameters from 3dFWHMx), -athr (per-voxel alpha), -pthr (per-voxel p thresholds)
3dClustSim.keyPoints: Use ACF-based smoothness (not FWHM) from 3dFWHMx on residuals. Updated in 2016 for non-Gaussian assumptions.
3dClustSim.typicalUse: Determining cluster size thresholds for multiple comparison correction.

nxyz.label: Size of 3D grid (n1 n2 n3, default 64 64 32)

dxyz.label: Voxel dimensions in mm (d1 d2 d3, default 3.5 3.5 3.5)

fwhm.label: Gaussian filter width in mm (not recommended)

3dFWHMx.name: 3dFWHMx
3dFWHMx.function: Estimates spatial smoothness of data using the autocorrelation function (ACF) model.
3dFWHMx.modality: Residual 4D NIfTI/AFNI from GLM analysis plus brain mask.
3dFWHMx.keyPoints: Run on residuals (not original data). ACF model accounts for non-Gaussian spatial structure. Output feeds into 3dClustSim.

mask.label: Use only nonzero voxels in mask

demed.label: Subtract median of each voxel time series

unif.label: Normalize voxel time series to same MAD

detrend.label: Remove polynomial trends up to order q

acf.label: Compute ACF fit (output a b c parameters)

ACF.label: Same as -acf but with comment lines

difMAD.label: Use first/second neighbor differences with MAD

3dMEMA.name: 3dMEMA
3dMEMA.function: Mixed effects meta-analysis for group studies that properly accounts for within and between-subject variance.
3dMEMA.modality: Subject-level beta and t-statistic volumes (3D NIfTI/AFNI).
3dMEMA.keyParameters: -set (group name and subject beta+tstat pairs), -groups (group names), -covariates (covariate file), -prefix (output)
3dMEMA.keyPoints: Uses both beta and t-stat from each subject. Better for unequal within-subject variance. Requires R.
3dMEMA.typicalUse: Group analysis with proper mixed effects modeling.

set.label: Data set specification (SETNAME subject beta_dset t_dset)

max_zeros.label: Skip voxels with more than N zero beta coefficients

model_outliers.label: Model outlier betas using Laplace distribution

residual_Z.label: Output residuals and Z-values for outliers

missing_data.label: Handle missing data (0 or file specification)

3dMVM.name: 3dMVM
3dMVM.function: Multivariate modeling framework supporting ANOVA/ANCOVA designs with between and within-subject factors.
3dMVM.modality: Subject-level 3D NIfTI/AFNI volumes with data table specifying factors.
3dMVM.keyPoints: Most flexible group analysis tool in AFNI. Handles complex repeated measures designs. Requires R.
3dMVM.typicalUse: Complex repeated measures and mixed designs.

table.label: Data table rows (Subj, Group, Cond, InputFile)

bsVars.label: Between-subjects factors and quantitative variables formula

mVar.label: Treat within-subject levels as simultaneous variables

3dLME.name: 3dLME
3dLME.function: Linear mixed effects modeling using R lme4 package for designs with random effects.
3dLME.modality: Subject-level 3D NIfTI/AFNI volumes with data table.
3dLME.keyPoints: Best for longitudinal data and nested designs. Uses R lme4 syntax. Handles missing data naturally.
3dLME.typicalUse: Longitudinal data, nested designs with random effects.

ML.label: Use Maximum Likelihood instead of REML

3dLMEr.name: 3dLMEr
3dLMEr.fullName: AFNI 3D Linear Mixed Effects with R (3dLMEr)
3dLMEr.function: Linear mixed effects with direct R formula syntax integration for flexible model specification.
3dLMEr.modality: Subject-level 3D NIfTI/AFNI volumes with data table.
3dLMEr.keyPoints: More flexible than 3dLME. Uses lmerTest for degrees of freedom. Accepts full R formula syntax.
3dLMEr.typicalUse: Flexible mixed effects with R formula syntax.

model.label: Fixed effects formula in R lmer syntax

ML.label: Use Maximum Likelihood instead of REML

3dNetCorr.name: 3dNetCorr
3dNetCorr.function: Computes pairwise correlation matrices between ROI time series extracted from a parcellation atlas.
3dNetCorr.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI/AFNI time series plus integer-labeled parcellation volume.
3dNetCorr.keyPoints: Outputs correlation matrix as text file. Use -fish_z for Fisher z-transformed values.
3dNetCorr.typicalUse: Creating functional connectivity matrices from parcellations.

in_rois.label: ROI mask with distinct integer labels

ts_out.label: Output mean time series per ROI

ts_label.label: Insert ROI label at start of each time series line

ts_indiv.label: Create directories with individual ROI time series

ts_wb_corr.label: Generate whole brain correlation maps per ROI

ts_wb_Z.label: Generate whole brain Fisher Z-score maps per ROI

weight_ts.label: Apply weights to ROI time series

weight_corr.label: Apply weights for weighted Pearson correlation

nifti.label: Output maps as NIFTI instead of BRIK/HEAD

push_thru_many_zeros.label: Continue even with >10% null time series in ROIs

allow_roi_zeros.label: Permit ROIs with all-zero time series

3dTcorr1D.name: 3dTcorr1D
3dTcorr1D.function: Computes voxelwise correlation between a 4D dataset and one or more 1D seed time series.
3dTcorr1D.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI/AFNI time series plus 1D seed time series file.
3dTcorr1D.keyPoints: Simple seed-based correlation. Extract seed time series first (e.g., with 3dmaskave).

dot.label: Calculate dot product instead of correlation

mask.label: Only process voxels nonzero in mask

short.label: Save results in scaled short format

3dTcorrMap.name: 3dTcorrMap
3dTcorrMap.function: Computes various whole-brain voxelwise correlation metrics including average correlation and global connectivity.
3dTcorrMap.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI/AFNI time series plus brain mask.
3dTcorrMap.typicalUse: Global connectivity metrics, whole-brain correlation exploration.

bpass.label: Bandpass frequencies L H in Hz

ort.label: Remove reference time series via regression

Gblur.label: Gaussian blur kernel width in mm

Mseed.label: Average seed over radius in mm

3dRSFC.name: 3dRSFC
3dRSFC.function: Computes resting-state frequency-domain metrics including ALFF, fALFF, mALFF, and RSFA from bandpass-filtered data.
3dRSFC.modality: 4D resting-state fMRI NIfTI/AFNI time series plus brain mask.
3dRSFC.keyPoints: Computes ALFF (amplitude of low-frequency fluctuations), fALFF (fractional ALFF), and RSFA.
3dRSFC.typicalUse: Amplitude of low-frequency fluctuations analysis in resting-state fMRI.

despike.label: Despike each time series before processing

ort.label: Orthogonalize input to columns in 1D file

dsort.label: Orthogonalize each voxel to matching voxel in dataset

dt.label: Set time step in seconds (default from header)

blur.label: Blur filter width FWHM in mm

localPV.label: Replace vectors with local principal vector (radius in mm)

norm.label: Make output time series have L2 norm = 1

no_rs_out.label: Skip time series output, calculate parameters only

3dROIstats.name: 3dROIstats
3dROIstats.function: Extracts statistical summary measures from data within defined ROI masks.
3dROIstats.modality: 3D or 4D NIfTI/AFNI volume plus ROI mask with integer labels.
3dROIstats.keyParameters: -mask (ROI mask), -nzmean (mean of non-zero voxels), -nzvoxels (count non-zero voxels), -minmax (min and max)
3dROIstats.keyPoints: Can handle multi-label ROI masks. Outputs one row per volume, one column per ROI.
3dROIstats.typicalUse: Extracting mean values from defined regions of interest.

mask_f2short.label: Convert float mask to short integers

roisel.label: Consider only ROIs listed in selection file

oneDformat.label: Output as 1D format with commented labels

3dmaskave.name: 3dmaskave
3dmaskave.function: Extracts and outputs the average time series from voxels within a mask region.
3dmaskave.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI/AFNI time series plus binary mask.
3dmaskave.keyParameters: -mask (mask dataset), -quiet (output values only), -mrange (min max value range in mask)
3dmaskave.keyPoints: Simple and fast ROI time series extraction. Output is one value per timepoint to stdout.
3dmaskave.typicalUse: Simple ROI time series extraction for connectivity analysis.

mindex.label: Which sub-brick from mask to use (default 0)

mrange.label: Restrict mask voxels to values between a and b

drange.label: Include only input voxels with values between a and b

slices.label: Restrict to slice numbers p through q

xbox.label: Create mask using box at x y z location

xball.label: Create mask using sphere at x y z with radius r

dump.label: Print all voxel values included in result

indump.label: Print voxel indexes for dumped values

3dUndump.name: 3dUndump
3dUndump.function: Creates a 3D dataset from a text file containing voxel coordinates and values.
3dUndump.modality: Text coordinate file plus master dataset for grid definition.
3dUndump.keyParameters: -prefix (output), -master (template grid), -xyz (coordinates are in mm), -srad (sphere radius in mm)
3dUndump.keyPoints: Use -srad to create spherical ROIs at each coordinate. Master dataset defines output grid.
3dUndump.typicalUse: Creating spherical ROIs from peak coordinates.

dimen.label: Set output dimensions (I J K voxels)

mask.label: Mask controlling which voxels receive values

dval.label: Default value for unspecified input voxels (default 1)

xyz.label: Input coordinates as (x,y,z) spatial mm coordinates

srad.label: Sphere radius around each input point

ROImask.label: Specify voxel values via ROI dataset labels

whereami.name: whereami
whereami.function: Reports anatomical atlas labels for given coordinates or identifies regions in multiple atlases simultaneously.
whereami.modality: MNI or Talairach coordinates, or labeled dataset.
whereami.keyPoints: Queries multiple atlases at once by default. Coordinates must match atlas space.
whereami.typicalUse: Identifying anatomical locations of activation peaks.

coord.label: Brain location in mm (x y z)

show_atlas_code.label: Show integer code to area label map

dset.label: Determine template space from reference dataset

min_prob.label: Minimum probability threshold for probabilistic atlases

bmask.label: Report overlap of non-zero voxels with atlas regions

3dresample.name: 3dresample
3dresample.function: Resamples a dataset to match the grid of another dataset or to a specified voxel size.
3dresample.keyPoints: Use -rmode NN for label/mask images, Li or Cu for continuous data.
3dresample.typicalUse: Matching resolution between datasets for analysis.

dxyz.label: Resample to new voxel dimensions (dx dy dz in mm)

orient.label: Reorient to new axis order (3-char code from APS, IRL)

rmode.label: Resampling method (NN=nearest neighbor, Li=linear, Cu=cubic, Bk=blocky)

upsample.label: Upsample voxels by factor (makes voxels smaller)

downsample.label: Downsample voxels by factor (makes voxels larger)

delta_scale.label: Generalized voxel size rescaling (<1 upsample, >1 downsample)

3dfractionize.name: 3dfractionize
3dfractionize.function: Resamples ROI/atlas datasets using fractional occupancy to maintain region representation at different resolutions.
3dfractionize.modality: ROI/atlas volume (3D NIfTI/AFNI) plus template for target grid.
3dfractionize.keyPoints: Better than nearest-neighbor for resampling parcellations. Preserves small ROIs better.

clip.label: Occupancy threshold (0-1 fraction, 1-100 percent, 100+ direct value)

preserve.label: Copy nonzero input values instead of creating fractional mask

3dcalc.name: 3dcalc
3dcalc.function: Voxelwise mathematical calculator supporting extensive expression syntax for operations on one or more datasets.
3dcalc.keyPoints: Extremely flexible expression syntax. Supports conditionals, trigonometric, and logical operations. Up to 26 inputs (a-z).
3dcalc.typicalUse: Mathematical operations, masking, thresholding, combining datasets.

fscale.label: Force scaling to maximum integer range

gscale.label: Force single scaling factor across all sub-bricks

usetemp.label: Use temporary file for intermediate results

3dTstat.name: 3dTstat
3dTstat.function: Computes voxelwise temporal statistics (mean, stdev, median, etc.) across a 4D time series.
3dTstat.keyPoints: Default computes mean. Can compute multiple statistics in one run.
3dTstat.typicalUse: Creating mean functional images, variance maps, temporal SNR.

l2norm.label: Compute L2 norm (sqrt of sum of squares)

mrange.label: Restrict mask values to range (a b)

3dinfo.name: 3dinfo
3dinfo.function: Displays header information and metadata from AFNI/NIfTI datasets.
3dinfo.modality: Any NIfTI or AFNI format dataset.
3dinfo.keyPoints: Essential for scripting and QC. Use specific flags for machine-readable output.
3dinfo.typicalUse: Quality control, scripting decisions based on data properties.

handedness.label: Return L or R for orientation

3dcopy.name: 3dcopy
3dcopy.function: Copies a dataset with optional format conversion between AFNI and NIfTI formats.
3dcopy.modality: Any NIfTI or AFNI format dataset.
3dcopy.keyPoints: Output format determined by extension. Simple way to convert between formats.
3dcopy.typicalUse: Format conversion between AFNI and NIfTI, making editable copies.

3dZeropad.name: 3dZeropad
3dZeropad.function: Adds zero-valued slices around dataset boundaries to extend the image matrix.
3dZeropad.keyParameters: -I/-S/-A/-P/-R/-L (add slices in each direction), -master (match grid of master dataset), -prefix (output)
3dZeropad.keyPoints: Use -master to match another dataset grid. Can also crop with negative values.
3dZeropad.typicalUse: Matching matrix sizes between datasets, preventing edge effects.

I.label: Add n planes at Inferior edge

S.label: Add n planes at Superior edge

A.label: Add n planes at Anterior edge

P.label: Add n planes at Posterior edge

L.label: Add n planes at Left edge

R.label: Add n planes at Right edge

z.label: Add planes on each z-axis face

RL.label: Symmetric padding to achieve n slices in R/L

AP.label: Symmetric padding to achieve n slices in A/P

IS.label: Symmetric padding to achieve n slices in I/S

pad2odds.label: Add 0 or 1 plane per R/A/S for odd slice counts

pad2evens.label: Add 0 or 1 plane per R/A/S for even slice counts

pad2mult.label: Make each axis a multiple of N

master.label: Match volume extents from reference dataset

3dNwarpApply.name: 3dNwarpApply
3dNwarpApply.function: Applies precomputed nonlinear warps (from 3dQwarp) to transform datasets.
3dNwarpApply.modality: 3D or 4D NIfTI/AFNI volume plus warp dataset.
3dNwarpApply.keyPoints: Can concatenate multiple warps in -nwarp string. Use wsinc5 interpolation for best quality.
3dNwarpApply.typicalUse: Applying 3dQwarp transformations to functional or other data.

master.label: Master dataset defining output grid (or WARP/NWARP)

newgrid.label: New grid spacing in mm for cubical voxels

suffix.label: Custom suffix for auto-generated output names

3dNwarpCat.name: 3dNwarpCat
3dNwarpCat.function: Concatenates multiple nonlinear warps and affine matrices into a single combined warp.
3dNwarpCat.modality: Multiple warp datasets and/or affine matrix files.
3dNwarpCat.keyParameters: -prefix (output), -warp1/-warp2/... (warps to concatenate), -iwarp (use inverse of a warp)
3dNwarpCat.keyPoints: Reduces interpolation artifacts from multiple separate applications. Can invert individual warps in the chain.
3dNwarpCat.typicalUse: Combining transformations efficiently for one-step resampling.

prefix.label: Output dataset prefix for concatenated warp

iwarp.label: Invert final output warp before writing

expad.label: Pad nonlinear warps by specified voxels

mri_convert.name: mri_convert
mri_convert.function: Converts between neuroimaging file formats (DICOM, NIfTI, MGH/MGZ, ANALYZE, etc.) with optional resampling and conforming.
mri_convert.keyParameters: --conform (resample to 256 cubed at 1mm isotropic), --out_type (output format), -vs (voxel size)
mri_convert.keyPoints: Use --conform to prepare T1 for FreeSurfer processing. Handles DICOM to NIfTI conversion. Can change voxel size and data type.
mri_convert.typicalUse: Converting DICOM to NIfTI, conforming images to FreeSurfer standards.

in_type.label: Input file format (cor, mgh, mgz, minc, analyze, nifti1, nii)

out_type.label: Output file format (cor, mgh, mgz, minc, analyze, nifti1, nii)

conform.label: Conform to 1mm voxel size in coronal slices

conform_min.label: Conform to minimum voxel direction size

conform_size.label: Conform to specified voxel size in mm

vox_size.label: Output voxel size (x y z) in mm

crop.label: Crop to 256 around center (x y z)

cropsize.label: Crop to specified size (dx dy dz)

fwhm.label: Smooth input volume by FWHM in mm

no_scale.label: Do not rescale values for COR

force_ras.label: Use default when orientation info absent

split.label: Split output frames into separate files

mri_watershed.name: mri_watershed
mri_watershed.function: Brain extraction using a hybrid watershed/surface deformation algorithm to find the brain-skull boundary.
mri_watershed.modality: T1-weighted 3D volume (typically MGZ format within FreeSurfer pipeline).
mri_watershed.keyParameters: -T1 (specify T1 volume), -atlas (use atlas for initial estimate), -h (preflooding height, default 25)
mri_watershed.keyPoints: Core component of recon-all. Adjust -h parameter if too much/too little brain removed. Usually part of autorecon1.

seed.label: Add seed point coordinates (x y z)

center.label: Brain center in voxels (x y z)

useSRAS.label: Use surface RAS instead of scanner RAS

noT1.label: Skip T1 analysis to conserve memory

mri_normalize.name: mri_normalize
mri_normalize.function: Normalizes T1 image intensities so that white matter has a target intensity value (default 110).
mri_normalize.modality: T1-weighted 3D volume (MGZ format, within FreeSurfer pipeline).
mri_normalize.keyParameters: -n (number of iterations), -b (bias field smoothing sigma), -aseg (use aseg for normalization regions)
mri_normalize.keyPoints: Part of recon-all autorecon1. Creates nu.mgz (non-uniformity corrected) and T1.mgz (intensity normalized).
mri_normalize.typicalUse: Preparing T1 for segmentation within FreeSurfer pipeline.

noaseg.label: Do not use aseg for normalization

mri_segment.name: mri_segment
mri_segment.function: Segments white matter from normalized T1 image using intensity thresholding and morphological operations.
mri_segment.keyPoints: Part of recon-all. Outputs wm.mgz used for surface reconstruction. Quality depends on good intensity normalization.
mri_segment.typicalUse: White matter identification for surface reconstruction.

nmin.label: Minimum number of white matter voxels

mris_inflate.name: mris_inflate
mris_inflate.function: Inflates folded cortical surface to a smooth shape while minimizing metric distortion for visualization.
mris_inflate.keyPoints: Creates inflated surface for visualizing buried cortex. Part of recon-all. Metric distortion encoded in sulc file.
mris_inflate.typicalUse: Creating inflated surfaces for visualization of cortical data.

w.label: Write intermediate surfaces every N iterations

mris_sphere.name: mris_sphere
mris_sphere.function: Maps the inflated cortical surface to a sphere for inter-subject spherical registration.
mris_sphere.keyPoints: Prerequisite for cortical atlas registration. Part of recon-all. Spherical mapping enables vertex-wise inter-subject comparisons.
mris_sphere.typicalUse: Preparing cortical surface for spherical registration and atlas labeling.

q.label: Omit self-intersection and vertex location info

mri_aparc2aseg.name: mri_aparc2aseg
mri_aparc2aseg.function: Combines surface-based cortical parcellation (aparc) with volumetric subcortical segmentation (aseg) into a single volume.
mri_aparc2aseg.keyPoints: Creates aparc+aseg.mgz combining ~80 cortical and subcortical regions. Different parcellation schemes available.
mri_aparc2aseg.typicalUse: Creating volumetric parcellation from surface labels for ROI analysis.

mri_annotation2label.name: mri_annotation2label
mri_annotation2label.function: Extracts individual region labels from a surface annotation file into separate label files.
mri_annotation2label.keyPoints: Creates one .label file per region. Label files contain vertex indices and coordinates.
mri_annotation2label.typicalUse: Extracting individual ROIs from parcellation for targeted analysis.

mris_ca_label.name: mris_ca_label
mris_ca_label.function: Applies a cortical parcellation atlas to an individual subject using trained classifier on spherical surface.
mris_ca_label.modality: FreeSurfer subject directory with sphere.reg (requires completed recon-all).
mris_ca_label.keyPoints: Uses Gaussian classifier atlas trained on manual labels. Part of recon-all. Different atlases available.
mris_ca_label.typicalUse: Applying cortical parcellation atlas to individual subjects.

novar.label: Do not use variance in classification

mri_label2vol.name: mri_label2vol
mri_label2vol.function: Converts surface-based labels to volumetric ROIs using a registration matrix.
mri_label2vol.modality: FreeSurfer label file plus template volume and registration.
mri_label2vol.keyPoints: Requires registration between surface and target volume space. Use --proj to control projection depth.
mri_label2vol.typicalUse: Creating volumetric ROIs from FreeSurfer surface parcellations.

proj.label: Projection method and parameters (frac/abs start stop delta)

label_voxel_volume.label: Fill with voxel volume instead of 1

bbregister.name: bbregister
bbregister.function: High-quality registration of functional EPI images to FreeSurfer anatomy using white matter boundary contrast.
bbregister.modality: 3D EPI volume (mean/example func) plus FreeSurfer subject directory.
bbregister.keyPoints: Superior to volume-based registration for EPI-to-T1. Requires completed recon-all. --init-fsl recommended.
bbregister.typicalUse: High-quality EPI to T1 registration using cortical surfaces.

no_coreg_ref_mask.label: Do not use aparc+aseg.mgz as reference mask

no_brute2.label: Disable brute force search on pass 2

no_fsl_bet_mov.label: Do not apply BET to moveable volume

mri_vol2surf.name: mri_vol2surf
mri_vol2surf.function: Projects volumetric data (fMRI, PET, etc.) onto the cortical surface using specified sampling method.
mri_vol2surf.modality: 3D or 4D NIfTI/MGZ volume plus FreeSurfer subject registration.
mri_vol2surf.keyPoints: Use --projfrac 0.5 to sample at mid-cortical depth. Can average across depths with --projfrac-avg.
mri_vol2surf.typicalUse: Mapping functional or PET data to cortical surface for surface-based analysis.

mri_surf2vol.name: mri_surf2vol
mri_surf2vol.function: Projects surface-based data back to volumetric space using registration and template volume.
mri_surf2vol.modality: FreeSurfer surface overlay file plus template volume and registration.
mri_surf2vol.keyPoints: Inverse of mri_vol2surf. Template defines output grid dimensions.
mri_surf2vol.typicalUse: Converting surface-based results back to volume space for reporting.

fill_projfrac.label: Fill between projection fractions (start stop delta)

mris_preproc.name: mris_preproc
mris_preproc.fullName: FreeSurfer Surface Data Preprocessing for Group Analysis
mris_preproc.function: Concatenates surface data across subjects onto a common template surface for group-level analysis.
mris_preproc.modality: Per-subject surface overlays (thickness, area, etc.) from FreeSurfer processing.
mris_preproc.keyPoints: Resamples all subjects to common surface (fsaverage). Can smooth on surface with --fwhm.
mris_preproc.typicalUse: Preparing surface data for group statistical analysis.

f.label: Text file with list of subjects

fwhm.label: Smooth on target surface by FWHM in mm

fwhm_src.label: Smooth on source surface by FWHM in mm

niters.label: Smooth by N nearest neighbor iterations

paired_diff_norm2.label: Paired diff normalized by first timepoint

mri_glmfit.name: mri_glmfit
mri_glmfit.function: Fits a general linear model on surface or volume data for group-level statistical analysis.
mri_glmfit.modality: Concatenated surface data from mris_preproc or stacked volume data.
mri_glmfit.keyParameters: --y (input data), --fsgd (FreeSurfer group descriptor), --C (contrast file), --surf (surface subject), --glmdir (output directory)
mri_glmfit.keyPoints: Uses FSGD file for design specification. Supports DODS and DOSS design types. Can run on surface or volume data.
mri_glmfit.typicalUse: Surface-based or volume-based group statistical analysis.

surface.label: Surface subject and hemisphere (e.g., fsaverage lh)

fwhm.label: Smooth input by FWHM in mm

var_fwhm.label: Smooth variance by FWHM in mm

mris_anatomical_stats.name: mris_anatomical_stats
mris_anatomical_stats.function: Computes surface-based morphometric measures (thickness, area, volume, curvature) for each region in a parcellation.
mris_anatomical_stats.modality: FreeSurfer subject directory with completed recon-all.
mris_anatomical_stats.keyPoints: Outputs per-region cortical thickness, surface area, gray matter volume, and curvature.
mris_anatomical_stats.typicalUse: Extracting regional cortical thickness, area, and volume measures.

mri_segstats.name: mri_segstats
mri_segstats.function: Computes volume and intensity statistics for each region in a segmentation volume.
mri_segstats.modality: Segmentation volume (e.g., aseg.mgz) plus optional intensity volume.
mri_segstats.keyPoints: Reports volume, mean intensity, and other statistics per region. Can use any segmentation volume.
mri_segstats.typicalUse: Extracting regional volumes and mean intensities per structure.

aparcstats2table.name: aparcstats2table
aparcstats2table.fullName: FreeSurfer Cortical Stats to Group Table
aparcstats2table.function: Collects cortical parcellation statistics across multiple subjects into a single table for group analysis.
aparcstats2table.modality: Multiple FreeSurfer subject directories with completed recon-all.
aparcstats2table.keyPoints: Creates one row per subject, one column per region. Output table ready for statistical software.
aparcstats2table.typicalUse: Creating group spreadsheet of cortical morphometry for statistical analysis.

common_parcs.label: Only output parcels common to all subjects

asegstats2table.name: asegstats2table
asegstats2table.fullName: FreeSurfer Subcortical Stats to Group Table
asegstats2table.function: Collects subcortical segmentation statistics across multiple subjects into a single table.
asegstats2table.modality: Multiple FreeSurfer subject directories with completed recon-all.
asegstats2table.keyParameters: --subjects (subject list), --meas (measure: volume, mean), --tablefile (output table), --stats (stats file name)
asegstats2table.keyPoints: Creates one row per subject with subcortical volumes. Default uses aseg.stats.

segids.label: File with segmentation IDs to extract

common_segs.label: Only output segs common to all subjects

dmri_postreg.name: dmri_postreg
dmri_postreg.function: Post-registration processing for diffusion MRI data as part of the TRACULA tractography pipeline.
dmri_postreg.modality: Registered diffusion MRI data within FreeSurfer/TRACULA directory structure.
dmri_postreg.keyPoints: Part of TRACULA pipeline. Handles diffusion-to-structural registration refinement. Usually called by trac-all.
dmri_postreg.typicalUse: Part of TRACULA pipeline for automated tractography.

N4BiasFieldCorrection.name: N4BiasFieldCorrection
N4BiasFieldCorrection.function: Advanced bias field (intensity inhomogeneity) correction using the N4 algorithm with iterative B-spline fitting.
N4BiasFieldCorrection.modality: 3D NIfTI volume (any MRI contrast), optional brain mask.
N4BiasFieldCorrection.keyPoints: Gold standard for bias correction. Use mask to restrict correction to brain. -s 4 speeds up computation.
N4BiasFieldCorrection.typicalUse: Removing intensity inhomogeneity before segmentation or registration.

mask_image.label: Binary mask to restrict correction region

weight_image.label: Weight image for voxel weighting during fitting

shrink_factor.label: Shrink factor for faster processing (1-4 typical)

DenoiseImage.name: DenoiseImage
DenoiseImage.function: Reduces noise in MRI images using an adaptive non-local means algorithm that preserves structural details.
DenoiseImage.keyPoints: Preserves edges better than Gaussian smoothing. Can output estimated noise image. Apply before bias correction.
DenoiseImage.typicalUse: Noise reduction while preserving structural edges.

mask_image.label: Mask image to limit denoising region

ImageMath.name: ImageMath
ImageMath.function: Versatile tool for image arithmetic, morphological operations, distance transforms, and various measurements.
ImageMath.keyPoints: Operations include: m (multiply), + (add), ME/MD (erode/dilate), GetLargestComponent, FillHoles, Normalize.
ImageMath.typicalUse: Mathematical operations, morphological operations, connected component analysis.

operation.label: Operation to perform (m, +, -, /, G, MD, ME, MO, MC, etc.)

second_input.label: Second input image for binary operations

scalar_value.label: Scalar value for operations (alternative to second image)

ThresholdImage.name: ThresholdImage
ThresholdImage.function: Applies various thresholding methods to create binary masks, including Otsu and k-means adaptive thresholding.
ThresholdImage.keyPoints: Otsu mode automatically finds optimal threshold. Binary mode uses explicit lower/upper bounds.
ThresholdImage.typicalUse: Creating binary masks, Otsu-based adaptive thresholding.

threshold_high.label: Upper threshold value (use inf for no upper bound)

inside_value.label: Value for voxels inside threshold range (default 1)

outside_value.label: Value for voxels outside threshold range (default 0)

LabelGeometryMeasures.name: LabelGeometryMeasures
LabelGeometryMeasures.function: Computes geometric properties (volume, centroid, bounding box, eccentricity) for each labeled region in a parcellation.
LabelGeometryMeasures.modality: 3D integer-labeled NIfTI volume (parcellation/segmentation), optional intensity image.
LabelGeometryMeasures.keyPoints: Outputs CSV with volume, centroid, elongation, roundness per label.
LabelGeometryMeasures.typicalUse: Extracting volume, centroid, and shape measures per labeled region.

intensity_image.label: Intensity image for weighted measures (use 'none' to skip)

antsJointLabelFusion.sh.name: antsJointLabelFusion.sh
antsJointLabelFusion.sh.function: Multi-atlas segmentation that combines labels from multiple pre-labeled atlases using joint label fusion with local weighting.
antsJointLabelFusion.sh.modality: Target 3D NIfTI volume plus multiple atlas images with corresponding label maps.
antsJointLabelFusion.sh.keyPoints: More accurate than single-atlas segmentation. Requires multiple registered atlases. Computationally intensive but highly accurate.
antsJointLabelFusion.sh.typicalUse: High-accuracy segmentation using multiple atlas priors.

atlas_labels.label: Atlas label images corresponding to each atlas

mask_image.label: Mask image for limiting fusion region

antsRegistration.name: antsRegistration
antsRegistration.function: State-of-the-art image registration supporting multiple stages (rigid, affine, SyN) with configurable metrics and convergence.
antsRegistration.modality: Any 3D NIfTI volume pair (fixed and moving images).
antsRegistration.keyPoints: Multi-stage approach: rigid then affine then SyN. SyN is symmetric diffeomorphic. CC metric best for intra-modal, MI for inter-modal.
antsRegistration.typicalUse: High-quality multi-stage registration with full parameter control.

use_float.label: Use float precision instead of double

antsRegistrationSyN.sh.name: antsRegistrationSyN.sh
antsRegistrationSyN.sh.function: Symmetric normalization registration with sensible default parameters for common registration tasks.
antsRegistrationSyN.sh.keyParameters: -d (dimension), -f (fixed), -m (moving), -o (output prefix), -t (transform type: s=SyN, b=BSplineSyN, a=affine only)
antsRegistrationSyN.sh.keyPoints: Good defaults for most use cases. Outputs forward/inverse warps and affine. Preferred over raw antsRegistration for simplicity.
antsRegistrationSyN.sh.typicalUse: Standard registration with good defaults for structural normalization.

transform_type.label: Transform type (t=translation, r=rigid, a=affine, s=SyN, b=b-spline SyN)

reproducible.label: Enable reproducible mode with fixed random seed

antsRegistrationSyNQuick.sh.name: antsRegistrationSyNQuick.sh
antsRegistrationSyNQuick.sh.function: Fast SyN registration with reduced iterations and coarser sampling for rapid approximate registration.
antsRegistrationSyNQuick.sh.keyPoints: Same interface as antsRegistrationSyN.sh but ~4x faster with slightly less accuracy.
antsRegistrationSyNQuick.sh.typicalUse: Quick registration when speed is priority over maximum accuracy.

transform_type.label: Transform type (t=translation, r=rigid, a=affine, s=SyN, b=b-spline SyN)

reproducible.label: Enable reproducible mode with fixed random seed

antsApplyTransforms.name: antsApplyTransforms
antsApplyTransforms.function: Applies one or more precomputed transformations (affine + warp) to images, applying transforms in reverse order of specification.
antsApplyTransforms.modality: 3D or 4D NIfTI volume plus transform files (affine .mat and/or warp .nii.gz).
antsApplyTransforms.keyPoints: Transforms applied in REVERSE order listed. Use -n NearestNeighbor for label images. -e flag for time series.
antsApplyTransforms.typicalUse: Applying registration transforms to data, labels, or ROIs.

transforms.label: Transform files (applied in reverse order - last specified first)

default_value.label: Default voxel value for out-of-bounds points

use_float.label: Use float instead of double for computations

antsMotionCorr.name: antsMotionCorr
antsMotionCorr.function: Motion correction for time series data using ANTs registration framework with rigid or affine transformations.
antsMotionCorr.modality: 4D fMRI or dynamic PET NIfTI time series.
antsMotionCorr.keyPoints: Can compute average image and motion-correct simultaneously. Uses ANTs optimization. Slower than MCFLIRT.
antsMotionCorr.typicalUse: High-quality motion correction using ANTs registration framework.

num_images.label: Number of images to use from time series

use_fixed_reference.label: Use fixed reference for all time points

antsIntermodalityIntrasubject.sh.name: antsIntermodalityIntrasubject.sh
antsIntermodalityIntrasubject.sh.function: Specialized registration between different imaging modalities within the same subject.
antsIntermodalityIntrasubject.sh.modality: Two different-modality volumes from the same subject (e.g., T1 + T2, or T1 + EPI).
antsIntermodalityIntrasubject.sh.keyPoints: Uses mutual information cost function appropriate for cross-modal registration.
antsIntermodalityIntrasubject.sh.typicalUse: T1-to-T2, fMRI-to-T1, or DWI-to-T1 within-subject alignment.

input_image.label: Input scalar image to match (e.g., b0 or pCASL)

template_prefix.label: Output prefix from prior antsRegistration T1-to-template

label_image.label: Label image in template space (e.g., AAL atlas)

Atropos.name: Atropos
Atropos.function: Probabilistic tissue segmentation using expectation-maximization algorithm with Markov random field spatial prior.
Atropos.modality: Brain-extracted 3D NIfTI volume plus brain mask.
Atropos.keyPoints: Initialize with KMeans[3] for basic GM/WM/CSF or use prior probability images. MRF prior improves spatial coherence.
Atropos.typicalUse: GMM-based brain tissue segmentation with spatial regularization.

use_euclidean_distance.label: Use Euclidean distance for label propagation

antsAtroposN4.sh.name: antsAtroposN4.sh
antsAtroposN4.sh.fullName: ANTs Combined Atropos with N4 Bias Correction
antsAtroposN4.sh.function: Iteratively combines N4 bias field correction with Atropos segmentation for improved results on biased images.
antsAtroposN4.sh.modality: Brain-extracted 3D NIfTI volume plus brain mask.
antsAtroposN4.sh.keyParameters: -d (dimension), -a (anatomical image), -x (mask), -c (number of classes), -o (output prefix), -n (number of iterations)
antsAtroposN4.sh.keyPoints: Iterative approach: N4 correction improves segmentation, which improves next N4 iteration. Superior to running separately.
antsAtroposN4.sh.typicalUse: Iterative N4 + segmentation for better results on images with bias field.

mask_image.label: Binary mask defining region of interest

atropos_iterations.label: Number of Atropos iterations per N4 iteration

use_euclidean_distance.label: Use Euclidean distance in distance prior

posterior_for_n4.label: Posterior labels for N4 weight mask

antsBrainExtraction.sh.name: antsBrainExtraction.sh
antsBrainExtraction.sh.function: High-quality brain extraction using registration to a brain template and tissue priors for robust skull stripping.
antsBrainExtraction.sh.modality: T1-weighted 3D NIfTI volume plus brain template and brain probability mask.
antsBrainExtraction.sh.keyPoints: More robust than BET for difficult cases. Requires template and prior. Slower but generally more accurate.
antsBrainExtraction.sh.typicalUse: High-quality skull stripping, especially for challenging datasets.

KellyKapowski.name: KellyKapowski
KellyKapowski.function: Estimates cortical thickness using the DiReCT algorithm from segmentation data.
KellyKapowski.modality: Tissue segmentation image plus GM and WM probability maps (3D NIfTI).
KellyKapowski.keyPoints: Core thickness estimation engine used by antsCorticalThickness.sh. Requires good segmentation as input.
KellyKapowski.typicalUse: Computing cortical thickness from pre-existing tissue segmentation.

antsCorticalThickness.sh.name: antsCorticalThickness.sh
antsCorticalThickness.sh.function: Complete automated pipeline for cortical thickness estimation using DiReCT, including brain extraction, segmentation, and registration.
antsCorticalThickness.sh.modality: T1-weighted 3D NIfTI volume plus brain template and tissue priors.
antsCorticalThickness.sh.keyParameters: -d (dimension), -a (anatomical image), -e (brain template), -m (brain probability mask), -p (tissue priors prefix), -o (output prefix)
antsCorticalThickness.sh.keyPoints: Runs full pipeline: N4, brain extraction, segmentation, registration, thickness. Requires template and priors. Computationally intensive.
antsCorticalThickness.sh.typicalUse: Complete DiReCT-based cortical thickness measurement pipeline.

segmentation_priors_dir.label: Directory containing segmentation priors (used with segmentation_priors pattern)

eddy.name: eddy
eddy.fullName: FSL Eddy Current and Motion Correction (eddy)
eddy.function: Corrects eddy current-induced distortions and subject movement in diffusion MRI data using a Gaussian process model.
eddy.modality: 4D diffusion-weighted NIfTI with b-values (.bval), b-vectors (.bvec), acquisition parameters, and index files.
eddy.keyPoints: Should follow topup if available. Outputs rotated bvecs to account for motion. Use --repol for outlier replacement. GPU version (eddy_cuda) much faster.
eddy.typicalUse: Primary preprocessing step for diffusion MRI after topup distortion correction.

index.label: Index file mapping volumes to acquisition parameters

dont_sep_offs_move.label: Do not separate field offset from subject movement

nvoxhp.label: Number of voxels for hyperparameter estimation (default 1000)

dtifit.name: dtifit
dtifit.function: Fits a diffusion tensor model to each voxel of preprocessed diffusion-weighted data to generate scalar diffusion maps.
dtifit.modality: 4D diffusion-weighted NIfTI with b-values (.bval), b-vectors (.bvec), and brain mask.
dtifit.keyPoints: Outputs FA, MD, eigenvalues (L1/L2/L3), eigenvectors (V1/V2/V3), and full tensor. Assumes single-fiber per voxel (use bedpostx for crossing fibers).
dtifit.typicalUse: Generating fractional anisotropy (FA) and mean diffusivity (MD) maps from DWI data.

bedpostx.name: bedpostx
bedpostx.fullName: Bayesian Estimation of Diffusion Parameters Obtained using Sampling Techniques (BEDPOSTX)
bedpostx.function: Multi-step pipeline for Bayesian estimation of fiber orientation distributions. Internally runs multi-fiber MCMC sampling across all voxels with automatic convergence checking, supporting multiple crossing fibers per voxel.
bedpostx.modality: Directory containing 4D DWI (data.nii.gz), b-values (bvals), b-vectors (bvecs), and brain mask (nodif_brain_mask.nii.gz).
