bedpostx.keyPoints: Very computationally intensive (hours-days). GPU version (bedpostx_gpu) strongly recommended. Required before probtrackx2. Outputs fiber orientations and uncertainty estimates.
bedpostx.typicalUse: Prerequisite for probabilistic tractography with probtrackx2.

data_dir.label: Input data directory (must contain data, bvals, bvecs, nodif_brain_mask)

tbss_1_preproc.name: tbss_1_preproc
tbss_1_preproc.function: Preprocesses FA images for TBSS by slightly eroding them and zeroing end slices to remove outlier voxels.
tbss_1_preproc.modality: FA maps (3D NIfTI) from dtifit, placed in a common directory.
tbss_1_preproc.keyPoints: Run from directory containing all subjects FA images. Creates FA/ subdirectory with preprocessed images. Must be run before tbss_2_reg.
tbss_1_preproc.typicalUse: First step of TBSS pipeline for voxelwise diffusion analysis.

tbss_2_reg.name: tbss_2_reg
tbss_2_reg.function: Registers all FA images to a target (best subject or standard-space template) using non-linear registration.
tbss_2_reg.keyParameters: -T (use FMRIB58_FA as target), -t <target> (use specified target), -n (find best subject as target)
tbss_2_reg.keyPoints: Use -T for standard target (recommended for most analyses). -n finds best representative subject but takes longer. Registration quality should be checked visually.
tbss_2_reg.typicalUse: Second step of TBSS pipeline: aligning all subjects to common space.

use_fmrib_target.label: Use FMRIB58_FA standard-space image as target

find_best_target.label: Find best target from all subjects

tbss_3_postreg.name: tbss_3_postreg
tbss_3_postreg.function: Creates mean FA image and FA skeleton by projecting registered FA data onto a mean tract center.
tbss_3_postreg.keyParameters: -S (use study-specific mean FA and skeleton), -T (use FMRIB58_FA mean and skeleton)
tbss_3_postreg.keyPoints: Creates mean_FA, mean_FA_skeleton, and all_FA (4D). Skeleton threshold typically 0.2 FA. -S recommended for study-specific analysis.
tbss_3_postreg.typicalUse: Third step of TBSS: creating the white matter skeleton for analysis.

study_specific.label: Derive mean FA and skeleton from study data

use_fmrib.label: Use FMRIB58_FA mean FA and skeleton

tbss_4_prestats.name: tbss_4_prestats
tbss_4_prestats.function: Projects all subjects FA data onto the mean FA skeleton, ready for voxelwise cross-subject statistics.
tbss_4_prestats.modality: Mean FA skeleton from tbss_3_postreg plus registered FA images.
tbss_4_prestats.keyPoints: Threshold determines which voxels are included in skeleton. Creates all_FA_skeletonised (4D) ready for randomise. Can also project non-FA data (MD, etc.) using tbss_non_FA.
tbss_4_prestats.typicalUse: Final TBSS step before statistical analysis with randomise.

oxford_asl.name: oxford_asl
oxford_asl.function: Multi-step pipeline for ASL MRI quantification. Internally chains motion correction, registration, BASIL kinetic modeling, calibration, and partial volume correction.
oxford_asl.modality: 4D ASL NIfTI (tag/control pairs) plus structural T1 image.
oxford_asl.keyParameters: -i (input ASL), -o (output dir), -s (structural image), --casl/--pasl (labeling type), --iaf (input format: tc/ct/diff), --tis (inversion times)
oxford_asl.keyPoints: Handles both pASL and CASL/pCASL. Performs kinetic modeling via BASIL internally. Use --wp for white paper quantification mode. Requires calibration image for absolute CBF.
oxford_asl.typicalUse: Complete ASL quantification from raw data to calibrated CBF maps.

spatial.label: Perform analysis with automatic spatial smoothing of CBF

casl.label: Data is CASL/pCASL (continuous ASL) rather than pASL

senscorr.label: Use bias field from segmentation for sensitivity correction

report.label: Report mean perfusion within GM mask (requires structural)

norm.label: Output perfusion maps normalised by GM mean (requires structural)

basil.name: basil
basil.fullName: Bayesian Inference for Arterial Spin Labeling (BASIL)
basil.function: Bayesian kinetic model inversion for ASL data using variational Bayes to estimate perfusion and arrival time.
basil.modality: 4D ASL NIfTI (differenced tag-control or raw tag/control pairs).
basil.keyParameters: -i (input ASL), -o (output dir), --tis (inversion times), --casl/--pasl, --bolus (bolus duration), --bat (arterial transit time prior)
basil.keyPoints: Core kinetic modeling engine used by oxford_asl. Multi-TI data enables arrival time estimation. Spatial regularization improves estimates in low-SNR regions.
basil.typicalUse: Bayesian perfusion quantification with uncertainty estimation.

nlls.label: Do least squares analysis as first step

asl_calib.name: asl_calib
asl_calib.function: Calibrates ASL perfusion data to absolute CBF units (ml/100g/min) using an M0 calibration image.
asl_calib.modality: Perfusion image (3D NIfTI) plus M0 calibration image and structural reference.
asl_calib.keyParameters: -i (perfusion image), -c (M0 calibration image), -s (structural image), --mode (voxelwise or reference region), --tr (TR of calibration)
asl_calib.keyPoints: Two modes: voxelwise (divides each voxel by local M0) or reference region (uses CSF M0 as reference). Reference region mode more robust to coil sensitivity variations.
asl_calib.typicalUse: Converting relative perfusion signals to absolute CBF values.

perfusion.label: CBF image for calibration (ASL native space)

mask.label: Reference mask in calibration image space

bmask.label: Brain mask (ASL space) for sensitivity or tissue T1 estimation

tissref.label: Tissue reference type (csf, wm, gm, none)

tr.label: TR used in calibration sequence (s, default 3.2, longtr mode)

cgain.label: Relative gain between calibration and ASL data (default 1, longtr mode)

t2star.label: Correct with T2* rather than T2

dwidenoise.name: dwidenoise
dwidenoise.function: Removes thermal noise from diffusion MRI data using Marchenko-Pastur PCA exploiting data redundancy across diffusion directions.
dwidenoise.modality: 4D diffusion-weighted NIfTI with multiple diffusion directions (minimum ~30 recommended).
dwidenoise.keyParameters: <input> <output>, -noise (output noise map), -extent (spatial patch size, default 5,5,5), -mask (brain mask)
dwidenoise.keyPoints: Should be run FIRST, before any other processing. Requires sufficient number of diffusion directions. Noise map useful for QC.
dwidenoise.typicalUse: First step in DWI preprocessing to improve SNR.

mrdegibbs.name: mrdegibbs
mrdegibbs.function: Removes Gibbs ringing artifacts (truncation artifacts) from MRI data using a local subvoxel-shift method.
mrdegibbs.keyParameters: <input> <output>, -axes (axes along which data was acquired, default 0,1)
mrdegibbs.keyPoints: Run after dwidenoise but before any interpolation-based processing. Only effective if data was NOT zero-filled in k-space.
mrdegibbs.typicalUse: Removing Gibbs ringing after denoising, before other preprocessing.

dwi2tensor.name: dwi2tensor
dwi2tensor.function: Estimates the diffusion tensor model at each voxel from preprocessed DWI data using weighted or ordinary least squares.
dwi2tensor.modality: 4D diffusion-weighted NIfTI with gradient table (b-values and b-vectors).
dwi2tensor.keyParameters: <input> <output>, -mask (brain mask), -b0 (output mean b=0 image), -dkt (output diffusion kurtosis tensor)
dwi2tensor.keyPoints: Assumes single fiber per voxel. Gradient information must be in image header or provided via -fslgrad bvecs bvals.
dwi2tensor.typicalUse: Fitting diffusion tensor to DWI data for FA/MD map generation.

iter.label: Number of iteratively-reweighted least squares iterations

tensor2metric.name: tensor2metric
tensor2metric.function: Extracts scalar metrics (FA, MD, AD, RD, eigenvalues, eigenvectors) from a fitted diffusion tensor image.
tensor2metric.modality: Diffusion tensor image (4D NIfTI from dwi2tensor).
tensor2metric.keyParameters: <input>, -fa (output FA map), -adc (output MD map), -ad (output AD), -rd (output RD), -vector (output eigenvectors)
tensor2metric.keyPoints: Multiple outputs can be generated in a single run. FA range 0-1. Specify each desired output explicitly.
tensor2metric.typicalUse: Generating FA, MD, and other scalar diffusion maps from tensor.

dwi2fod.name: dwi2fod
dwi2fod.function: Estimates fiber orientation distributions (FODs) using constrained spherical deconvolution to resolve crossing fibers.
dwi2fod.modality: 4D diffusion-weighted NIfTI with multi-shell or single-shell data, plus tissue response functions.
dwi2fod.keyPoints: Use msmt_csd for multi-shell data (recommended), csd for single-shell. Response functions from dwi2response.
dwi2fod.typicalUse: Estimating fiber orientations for subsequent tractography.

gm_fod.label: Output GM FOD filename (required before CSF)

csf_fod.label: Output CSF FOD filename (requires GM inputs)

tckgen.name: tckgen
tckgen.function: Generates streamline tractograms using various algorithms (iFOD2, FACT, etc.) from FOD or tensor images.
tckgen.modality: FOD image (from dwi2fod) or tensor image, plus optional seed/mask/ROI images.
tckgen.keyParameters: <source> <output.tck>, -algorithm (iFOD2, FACT, etc.), -seed_image (seeding region), -select (target streamline count), -cutoff (FOD amplitude cutoff)
tckgen.keyPoints: iFOD2 (default) is probabilistic and handles crossing fibers. Use -select for target count. -cutoff controls termination.

tcksift.name: tcksift
tcksift.function: Filters tractograms to improve biological plausibility by matching streamline density to FOD lobe integrals.
tcksift.modality: Tractogram (.tck) plus FOD image used for tractography.
tcksift.keyPoints: Dramatically improves connectome quantification. Run after tckgen. SIFT2 (tcksift2) outputs weights instead of filtering.
tcksift.typicalUse: Improving tractogram biological accuracy before connectome construction.

term_ratio.label: Target ratio of streamlines to keep

tck2connectome.name: tck2connectome
tck2connectome.function: Constructs a structural connectivity matrix by counting streamlines connecting pairs of regions from a parcellation.
tck2connectome.keyPoints: Output is NxN matrix. Use SIFT/SIFT2 filtered tractogram for quantitative connectomics. -symmetric recommended.
tck2connectome.typicalUse: Building structural connectivity matrices from tractography and parcellation.

assignment_radial_search.label: Radial search distance for node assignment (mm)

zero_diagonal.label: Zero the diagonal of the matrix

fmriprep.name: fmriprep
fmriprep.function: Automated, robust preprocessing pipeline for task-based and resting-state fMRI, combining tools from FSL, FreeSurfer, ANTs, and AFNI with best practices.
fmriprep.modality: BIDS-formatted dataset containing T1w anatomical and BOLD fMRI data (NIfTI format).
fmriprep.keyPoints: Requires BIDS-formatted input. Handles brain extraction, segmentation, registration, motion correction, distortion correction, and confound estimation. Generates comprehensive visual QC reports.
fmriprep.typicalUse: Complete standardized fMRI preprocessing from BIDS data to analysis-ready outputs.

mriqc.name: mriqc
mriqc.function: Multi-step quality control pipeline. Internally chains brain extraction, spatial normalization, and computation of dozens of image quality metrics (SNR, CNR, EFC, FBER, motion) with visual report generation.
mriqc.modality: BIDS-formatted dataset containing T1w, T2w, and/or BOLD fMRI data (NIfTI format).
mriqc.keyParameters: <bids_dir> <output_dir> participant, --participant-label (subject IDs), --modalities (T1w, T2w, bold), --no-sub (skip submission to web API)
mriqc.keyPoints: Requires BIDS-formatted input. Computes dozens of IQMs (SNR, CNR, EFC, FBER, motion metrics). Generates individual and group-level visual reports.
mriqc.typicalUse: Automated quality assessment of MRI data before preprocessing.

mri_gtmpvc.name: mri_gtmpvc
mri_gtmpvc.fullName: FreeSurfer Geometric Transfer Matrix Partial Volume Correction
mri_gtmpvc.function: Performs partial volume correction for PET data using the geometric transfer matrix method based on high-resolution anatomical segmentation.
mri_gtmpvc.modality: PET volume (3D NIfTI/MGZ) plus FreeSurfer segmentation (aparc+aseg).
mri_gtmpvc.keyParameters: --i (input PET), --seg (segmentation), --reg (registration to anatomy), --psf (point spread function FWHM in mm), --o (output directory)
mri_gtmpvc.keyPoints: Accounts for PET spatial resolution blurring across tissue boundaries. PSF should match scanner resolution (~4-6mm).
mri_gtmpvc.typicalUse: Partial volume correction of PET data using anatomical segmentation.

default_seg_merge.label: Use default scheme to merge hemispheres and set tissue types

ctab_default.label: Use default FreeSurfer color table with tissue types

vg_thresh.label: Volume geometry threshold for registration check

tbss_non_FA.name: tbss_non_FA
tbss_non_FA.function: Projects non-FA diffusion images (MD, AD, RD, etc.) onto the mean FA skeleton using the same registration from the FA-based TBSS pipeline.
tbss_non_FA.modality: Non-FA diffusion scalar maps (3D NIfTI) in same space as FA images used for TBSS.
tbss_non_FA.keyParameters: <non_FA_image> (e.g., all_MD) - run after tbss_4_prestats with non-FA data in stats directory
tbss_non_FA.keyPoints: Must run full TBSS pipeline on FA first. Non-FA images must be in same native space as original FA. Creates all_<measure>_skeletonised for use with randomise.
tbss_non_FA.typicalUse: Analyzing MD, AD, RD, or other diffusion metrics on the FA-derived skeleton.

measure.label: Non-FA measure name (e.g., MD, AD, RD, L1, L2, L3)

fa_directory.label: FA directory from TBSS pipeline (contains per-subject FA images and warps)

measure_directory.label: Directory containing per-subject non-FA images (filenames must match FA subjects without _FA suffix)

applytopup.name: applytopup
applytopup.function: Applies the susceptibility-induced off-resonance field estimated by topup to correct distortions in EPI images.
applytopup.modality: 3D or 4D EPI NIfTI plus topup output (movpar.txt and fieldcoef files).
applytopup.keyParameters: --imain (input images), --topup (topup output prefix), --datain (acquisition parameters), --inindex (index into datain), --out (output), --method (jac or lsr)
applytopup.keyPoints: Use after running topup. --method=jac applies Jacobian modulation (recommended for fMRI). Can apply to multiple images at once.
applytopup.typicalUse: Applying distortion correction to fMRI or DWI data using pre-computed topup results.

topup_fieldcoef.label: Topup field coefficients file (_fieldcoef.nii.gz from topup)

topup_movpar.label: Topup movement parameters file (_movpar.txt from topup)

encoding_file.label: Acquisition parameters file (same as used for topup)

inindex.label: Comma-separated indices into encoding_file for each input image

fsl_prepare_fieldmap.name: fsl_prepare_fieldmap
fsl_prepare_fieldmap.function: Prepares a fieldmap for use with FUGUE by converting phase difference images to radians per second.
fsl_prepare_fieldmap.modality: Phase difference image and magnitude image from gradient echo fieldmap acquisition.
fsl_prepare_fieldmap.keyPoints: Scanner type determines unwrapping method (SIEMENS most common). Delta TE is the echo time difference in milliseconds. Output is in rad/s for use with FUGUE.
fsl_prepare_fieldmap.typicalUse: Converting raw fieldmap images to FUGUE-compatible format for EPI distortion correction.

nocheck.label: Suppress sanity checking of image size/range/dimensions

prelude.name: prelude
prelude.fullName: FSL Phase Region Expanding Labeller for Unwrapping Discrete Estimates (PRELUDE)
prelude.function: Performs 3D phase unwrapping on wrapped phase images using a region-growing algorithm.
prelude.modality: Wrapped phase image (3D NIfTI) plus optional magnitude image for masking.
prelude.keyParameters: -p (wrapped phase), -a (magnitude for mask), -o (output unwrapped phase), -m (brain mask), -f (apply phase filter)
prelude.keyPoints: Essential preprocessing for fieldmap-based distortion correction. Magnitude image improves unwrapping quality. Can handle phase wraps > 2pi.
prelude.typicalUse: Unwrapping phase images before fieldmap calculation for distortion correction.

complex_input.label: Complex input image (alternative to phase/magnitude pair)

num_partitions.label: Number of phase partitions for initial labeling

bianca.name: bianca
bianca.function: Automated white matter hyperintensity (WMH) segmentation using supervised machine learning (k-nearest neighbor) trained on manually labeled data.
bianca.modality: T1-weighted and FLAIR images (3D NIfTI), plus training data with manual WMH masks.
bianca.keyParameters: --singlefile (input file list), --labelfeaturenum (which feature is the manual label), --brainmaskfeaturenum (brain mask feature), --querysubjectnum (subject to segment), --trainingnums (training subjects)
bianca.keyPoints: Requires training data with manual WMH labels. Uses spatial and intensity features. Performance depends on training data quality and similarity to test data.
bianca.typicalUse: Automated white matter lesion segmentation in aging, small vessel disease, or MS studies.

singlefile.label: Master file listing subjects, images, masks, and transformations

training_data.label: Directory containing all subject data files referenced in master file

querysubjectnum.label: Row number in master file for the subject to segment

brainmaskfeaturenum.label: Column number in master file containing brain mask

labelfeaturenum.label: Column number in master file containing manual lesion mask

featuresubset.label: Comma-separated column numbers for intensity features

matfeaturenum.label: Column number containing MNI transformation matrices

trainingpts.label: Max lesion training points per subject (number or "equalpoints")

saveclassifierdata.label: Save training data to specified file

loadclassifierdata.label: Load pre-saved classifier data from file

robustfov.name: robustfov
robustfov.fullName: FSL Robust Field of View Reduction
robustfov.function: Automatically identifies and removes neck/non-brain tissue by estimating the brain center and reducing the field of view to a standard size.
robustfov.keyParameters: -i (input), -r (output ROI volume), -m (output transformation matrix), -b (brain size estimate in mm, default 170)
robustfov.keyPoints: Useful for images with extensive neck coverage. Run before BET for more robust brain extraction. Does not resample, just crops.
robustfov.typicalUse: Preprocessing step before brain extraction to remove neck and improve BET robustness.

recon-all.name: recon-all
recon-all.function: Fully automated pipeline for cortical surface reconstruction and parcellation, including skull stripping, segmentation, surface tessellation, topology correction, inflation, registration, and parcellation.
recon-all.modality: T1-weighted 3D NIfTI or DICOM. Optional T2w or FLAIR for pial surface refinement.
recon-all.keyParameters: -s (subject ID), -i (input T1w), -T2 (T2w image for pial), -FLAIR (FLAIR for pial), -all (run full pipeline), -autorecon1/-autorecon2/-autorecon3 (run specific stages)
recon-all.keyPoints: Runtime 6-24 hours per subject. Creates cortical surfaces (white, pial), parcellations (Desikan-Killiany, Destrieux), subcortical segmentation (aseg), and morphometric measures. Use -T2pial or -FLAIRpial for improved pial surface placement.
recon-all.typicalUse: Complete cortical reconstruction for surface-based morphometry, parcellation-based analysis, and as prerequisite for fMRI surface analysis.

autorecon1.label: Run autorecon1 (motion correction, skull stripping, Talairach)

autorecon2.label: Run autorecon2 (segmentation, surface generation, topology fix)

t2_image.label: T2-weighted image for pial surface refinement

flair_image.label: FLAIR image for pial surface refinement

t2pial.label: Use T2 image for pial surface placement

flair_pial.label: Use FLAIR image for pial surface placement

wb_command_cifti_create_dense_timeseries.name: wb_command_cifti_create_dense_timeseries
wb_command_cifti_create_dense_timeseries.fullName: Connectome Workbench CIFTI Dense Timeseries Creation
wb_command_cifti_create_dense_timeseries.function: Creates a CIFTI dense timeseries file (.dtseries.nii) combining cortical surface data with subcortical volume data in a single grayordinates representation.
wb_command_cifti_create_dense_timeseries.modality: Surface GIFTI files (left/right hemisphere) plus subcortical volume NIfTI, or volume-only input.
wb_command_cifti_create_dense_timeseries.keyPoints: Core format for HCP-style analysis. Combines cortical surfaces and subcortical volumes. Standard grayordinate space is 91k (32k per hemisphere + subcortical).
wb_command_cifti_create_dense_timeseries.typicalUse: Creating CIFTI format fMRI data for HCP-style surface-based analysis.

volume_data.label: Volume file containing all voxel data for volume structures

left_metric.label: Metric file for left cortical surface

roi_left.label: ROI of vertices to use from left surface

right_metric.label: Metric file for right cortical surface

roi_right.label: ROI of vertices to use from right surface

timestep.label: Time step between frames in seconds (default 1.0)

wb_command_cifti_separate.name: wb_command_cifti_separate
wb_command_cifti_separate.function: Extracts surface or volume components from a CIFTI file into separate GIFTI metric or NIfTI volume files.
wb_command_cifti_separate.keyPoints: Opposite of cifti-create operations. Useful for extracting data for tools that do not support CIFTI format.
wb_command_cifti_separate.typicalUse: Extracting surface or volume data from CIFTI files for further processing.

volume_all.label: Output volume file for all volume structures

metric_left.label: Output metric file for left cortex

metric_right.label: Output metric file for right cortex

wb_command_cifti_smoothing.name: wb_command_cifti_smoothing
wb_command_cifti_smoothing.function: Applies geodesic Gaussian smoothing to CIFTI data on cortical surfaces and Euclidean smoothing in subcortical volumes.
wb_command_cifti_smoothing.modality: CIFTI dense file plus surface files for each hemisphere.
wb_command_cifti_smoothing.keyPoints: Surface smoothing follows cortical geometry (geodesic). Typical kernel 4-6mm FWHM. -fix-zeros prevents smoothing across medial wall.
wb_command_cifti_smoothing.typicalUse: Spatial smoothing of fMRI data in CIFTI format for HCP-style pipelines.

surface_kernel.label: Gaussian surface smoothing kernel size in mm (sigma unless -fwhm)

volume_kernel.label: Gaussian volume smoothing kernel size in mm (sigma unless -fwhm)

fwhm.label: Interpret kernel sizes as FWHM instead of sigma

fix_zeros_volume.label: Treat volume zeros as missing data

fix_zeros_surface.label: Treat surface zeros as missing data

wb_command_metric_smoothing.name: wb_command_metric_smoothing
wb_command_metric_smoothing.function: Applies geodesic Gaussian smoothing to surface metric data following the cortical surface geometry.
wb_command_metric_smoothing.modality: Surface GIFTI (.surf.gii) plus metric GIFTI (.func.gii or .shape.gii).
wb_command_metric_smoothing.keyPoints: Smoothing follows cortical folding pattern rather than 3D Euclidean distance. ROI can restrict smoothing to specific regions.
wb_command_metric_smoothing.typicalUse: Smoothing surface-based data (thickness, curvature, fMRI) for visualization or statistics.

smoothing_kernel.label: Gaussian smoothing kernel size in mm (sigma unless -fwhm)

fwhm.label: Interpret kernel size as FWHM instead of sigma

fix_zeros.label: Treat zero values as missing data

corrected_areas.label: Vertex areas metric for group average surfaces

wb_command_surface_sphere_project_unproj.name: wb_command_surface_sphere_project_unproject
wb_command_surface_sphere_project_unproj.function: Applies MSM or FreeSurfer spherical registration by projecting coordinates through registered sphere to target space.
wb_command_surface_sphere_project_unproj.keyPoints: Core operation for applying surface-based registration. Used to resample surfaces to different template spaces (fsaverage, fs_LR).
wb_command_surface_sphere_project_unproj.typicalUse: Applying surface registration transforms to resample data between atlas spaces.

sphere_in.label: Input sphere with desired output mesh

sphere_unproject_from.label: sphere-project-to deformed to the desired output space

amico_noddi.name: amico_noddi
amico_noddi.function: Fits the NODDI (Neurite Orientation Dispersion and Density Imaging) model to multi-shell diffusion MRI data using convex optimization for fast and robust estimation.
amico_noddi.modality: Multi-shell diffusion MRI (4D NIfTI) with b-values and b-vectors, plus brain mask.
amico_noddi.keyPoints: Requires multi-shell acquisition (recommended: b=0,1000,2000 s/mm2). Outputs NDI (neurite density), ODI (orientation dispersion), and fISO (isotropic fraction). Much faster than original NODDI MATLAB toolbox.
amico_noddi.typicalUse: Microstructural imaging for neurite density and orientation dispersion in white matter.

parallel_diffusivity.label: Intracellular diffusivity parallel to neurites in mm^2/s (default 0.0017)

fsl_regfilt.name: fsl_regfilt
fsl_regfilt.function: Removes nuisance components from 4D fMRI data by regressing out specified columns from a design or mixing matrix.
fsl_regfilt.modality: 4D fMRI NIfTI time series with associated ICA mixing matrix.
fsl_regfilt.keyPoints: Typically used with MELODIC output. Removes ICA components classified as noise. Component indices are comma-separated or ranges.
fsl_regfilt.typicalUse: Removing ICA-identified noise components from fMRI data, often used with FIX or manual classification.

freq_ic.label: Frequency IC file for frequency-based filtering

fslchfiletype.name: fslchfiletype
fslchfiletype.function: Converts neuroimaging files between NIfTI and ANALYZE file formats.
fslchfiletype.keyPoints: Supports NIFTI_GZ (.nii.gz), NIFTI (.nii), NIFTI_PAIR (.hdr/.img), and ANALYZE formats. Quick utility for format interoperability.
fslchfiletype.typicalUse: Converting between compressed and uncompressed NIfTI formats, or to legacy ANALYZE format.

fslhd.name: fslhd
fslhd.function: Displays NIfTI/ANALYZE image header information including dimensions, voxel sizes, data type, and orientation.
fslhd.keyPoints: Reports full header including sform/qform matrices, intent codes, and auxiliary info. XML output useful for programmatic parsing.
fslhd.typicalUse: Inspecting image metadata for quality control and debugging preprocessing issues.

fslinfo.name: fslinfo
fslinfo.function: Displays concise image dimension and voxel size information from a NIfTI header.
fslinfo.keyPoints: Compact output showing data type, dimensions (dim1-dim4), voxel sizes (pixdim1-pixdim4), and time step. Simpler than fslhd.
fslinfo.typicalUse: Quick inspection of image dimensions and voxel sizes for pipeline verification.

3dDWItoDT.name: 3dDWItoDT
3dDWItoDT.function: Fits a diffusion tensor model to DWI data using linear or nonlinear methods, outputting tensor components, eigenvalues, and eigenvectors.
3dDWItoDT.modality: 4D diffusion-weighted dataset with gradient vector file.
3dDWItoDT.keyPoints: Supports linear (default) and nonlinear fitting. Use -eigs for eigenvalue/eigenvector output required by 3dTrackID. Gradient file needs 3 columns per direction.
3dDWItoDT.typicalUse: Computing diffusion tensor and derived scalar maps (FA, MD) from DWI data in AFNI workflows.

gradient_file.label: Gradient vector file (3 columns per direction)

automask.label: Automatically compute brain mask from data

sep_dsets.label: Output separate datasets for each DTI parameter (FA, MD, V1, etc.)

max_iter.label: Maximum number of iterations for nonlinear fit

3dDWUncert.name: 3dDWUncert
3dDWUncert.function: Estimates uncertainty of diffusion tensor parameters via jackknife or bootstrap resampling of DWI data.
3dDWUncert.modality: 4D diffusion-weighted dataset with gradient vector file.
3dDWUncert.keyPoints: Provides confidence intervals for FA and eigenvector directions. Output used as input for probabilistic tractography with 3dTrackID. Computationally intensive.
3dDWUncert.typicalUse: Generating uncertainty estimates for probabilistic tractography in AFNI diffusion pipelines.

3dTrackID.name: 3dTrackID
3dTrackID.function: Performs deterministic, mini-probabilistic, or full probabilistic white matter tractography using diffusion tensor data.
3dTrackID.modality: DTI parameter volumes (from 3dDWItoDT with -eigs) and integer-labeled ROI mask.
3dTrackID.keyPoints: Three tracking modes with different speed/accuracy tradeoffs. Outputs tract files, connectivity matrices, and statistics. Requires 3dDWItoDT output with -eigs flag.
3dTrackID.typicalUse: White matter tractography and structural connectivity analysis in AFNI diffusion pipelines.

dti_in.label: DTI input prefix (basename, files staged via dti_files)

dti_files.label: DTI parameter files from 3dDWItoDT (HEAD/BRIK pairs, staged into working dir)

alg_Nseed_X.label: Number of seeds per voxel (X direction)

alg_Nseed_Y.label: Number of seeds per voxel (Y direction)

alg_Nseed_Z.label: Number of seeds per voxel (Z direction)

no_indipair_out.label: Do not output individual pairwise maps

dcm2niix.name: dcm2niix
dcm2niix.function: Converts DICOM medical images to NIfTI format with BIDS-compatible JSON sidecar files, preserving acquisition metadata.
dcm2niix.modality: DICOM image directory from any MRI acquisition.
dcm2niix.keyPoints: Generates BIDS-compatible JSON sidecars. Handles multi-echo, diffusion (bval/bvec), and Philips/Siemens/GE formats. Default filename format is %f_%p_%t_%s.
dcm2niix.typicalUse: First step in any neuroimaging pipeline: converting raw DICOM acquisitions to analysis-ready NIfTI.

ICA_AROMA.name: ICA_AROMA
ICA_AROMA.fullName: ICA-based Automatic Removal of Motion Artifacts (ICA-AROMA)
ICA_AROMA.function: Multi-step denoising pipeline for fMRI motion artifact removal. Internally chains MELODIC ICA decomposition, motion artifact feature extraction, classifier-based component labeling, and non-aggressive denoising.
ICA_AROMA.modality: Preprocessed 4D fMRI NIfTI in standard (MNI) space with motion parameters.
ICA_AROMA.keyPoints: Requires data in MNI space. Classifier uses four features: max RP correlation, edge fraction, HFC spatial fraction, and CSF fraction. Non-aggressive denoising recommended.
ICA_AROMA.typicalUse: Removing motion artifacts from resting-state or task fMRI data after standard preprocessing.

input.label: Input 4D fMRI data (preprocessed, in standard space)

TR.label: TR in seconds (extracted from data if not provided)

modalityDescriptions.Structural MRI: T1/T2-weighted imaging for brain anatomy. Used for skull-stripping, tissue segmentation, cortical reconstruction, and spatial registration.
modalityDescriptions.Functional MRI: BOLD-contrast imaging for brain activity. Used for motion correction, slice timing, spatial smoothing, temporal filtering, and statistical modeling.
modalityDescriptions.Diffusion MRI: Diffusion-weighted imaging for white matter microstructure. Used for eddy current correction, tensor fitting, tractography, and connectivity analysis.
modalityDescriptions.Arterial Spin Labeling: Non-invasive perfusion imaging using magnetically labeled blood. Used for cerebral blood flow quantification and partial volume correction.
modalityDescriptions.PET: Positron Emission Tomography for molecular imaging. Used for kinetic modeling, partial volume correction, and tracer-specific quantification.
modalityDescriptions.Multimodal: Cross-modality analysis pipelines. Tools that integrate structural, functional, and diffusion data for comprehensive brain mapping.
modalityDescriptions.Utilities: General-purpose neuroimaging utilities. Used for format conversion, image math, resampling, and quality control.
modalityDescriptions.Pipelines: Complete multi-step analysis pipelines that chain several operations internally. Each tool orchestrates preprocessing, registration, segmentation, or analysis stages as a single command.
